Steps to reproduce:

1. Add `ddtrace.contrib.django` to `INSTALLED_APPS` in your Django settings.
2. Launch Django shell: `python manage.py shell`
3. A rogue error message appears - `No handlers could be found for logger "ddtrace.writer"`

It will also appear every time you run something that initialises Django (like a celery app) and loads the ddtrace Django app without using `ddtrace-run`.

If you add `logging.getLogger('ddtrace.writer').addHandler(logging.NullHandler())` somewhere in your codebase there will be no log error.

It's and identical issue like this one: https://github.com/DataDog/datadogpy/issues/130

issue_title: Some Vertica 'UNION ALL' queries need brackets
issue_body:
Currently PyPika creates union queries as `query1 UNION ALL query2`.

This works fine in most cases, e.g. 

```
SELECT id FROM myTable
UNION ALL
SELECT id FROM myTable
```

At least for Vertica though, there are some queries where this throws an error, e.g.:

```
SELECT id FROM myTable ORDER BY id ASC
UNION ALL
SELECT id FROM myTable ORDER BY id ASC
```

The fix is as simple as adding brackets:

```
(SELECT id FROM myTable ORDER BY id ASC)
UNION ALL
(SELECT id FROM myTable ORDER BY id ASC)
```

issue_title: Cleaning up locating windows
issue_body:
How to locate windows is pretty complicated and not very well documented. There are also lot of undocumented and untested features. This issue covers cleaning up the functionality and, very importantly, documenting it adequately.

As part of the cleanup, undocumented features that are not considered useful are deprecated or removed. Such changes are obviously backwards incompatible, but because these are undocumented features, it's very unlikely that they are used widely. This is the functionality that is going to change:

- Using Python `None` or string `null` or empty string as a locator to select the main window is deprecated. Use documented `main` (default) instead.
- Using `popup` to select the latest new window is deprecated. Use documented `new` instead.
- Using `self` to select the current window is deprecated. Use earlier undocumented but much more explicit `current` instead.
- Locating windows by name, title or URL is not case-insensitive anymore.
- Specifying explicit locator strategy is not case-insensitive anymore.

There area also some enhancements:

- New keyword `Get Window Handles` is added to replace `List Windows` later. The old keyword is "silently deprecated" at this point.
- Explicit locator strategy can be specified using `strategy:value` syntax in addition to `strategy=value` (see also #908).

```python
from datetime import datetime

The NavX works fine as far as I can tell (I've only tested `getAngle()`). But these constant errors make it difficult to debug any other problems.

issue_title: Missing `pipenv help` subcommand
issue_body:
Apologies if this has been suggested before, but I couldn't find it in the existing issues.

Given that pipenv supports subcommands such as `pipenv run`, `pipenv install`, ..., the first thing I try when I'm unsure about something is `pipenv help`. However, this results in an error ('No such command "help".') It always takes a few second to realise that `pipenv -h` will do the trick but it is a little confusing and results in a mental context switch from the task at hand that would be nice to avoid.

I know that @kennethreitz said in [another issue](https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/issues/485) that he wants to keep the list of commands as small as possible, but the `help` command is arguably one of the most fundamental ones and it is surprising to a user that `pipenv help` doesn't work while something like `pipenv install` does.

Suggestion:

- Add `pipenv help` (equivalent to `pipenv -h`)
- Add `pipenv help <subcommand>` (equivalent to `pipenv <subcommand> -h`), for instance: `pipenv help run`

issue_title: Classifiers do not make a good config section
issue_body:
So I see that Setuptools 30.3.0 added support for defining package metadata in `setup.cfg` instead of `setup.py`.  Neat!  The documentation even gives an example of how you could define a package's classifiers in a section of their own:

```
[metadata.classifiers]
Framework :: Django
Programming Language :: Python :: 3.5
```

Let me try!  I'll just create a fresh virtualenv with Setuptools 34.1.0 already installed, using either Python 2.7.12 or 3.5.2 (same results either way).  Now if I move my classifiers to `setup.cfg` like so:

```
[metadata.classifiers]
Development Status :: 3 - Alpha
Programming Language :: Python :: 2
Programming Language :: Python :: 2.6
Programming Language :: Python :: 2.7
Programming Language :: Python :: 3
Programming Language :: Python :: 3.3
Programming Language :: Python :: 3.4
Programming Language :: Python :: 3.5
Programming Language :: Python :: 3.6
Programming Language :: Python :: Implementation :: CPython
Programming Language :: Python :: Implementation :: PyPy
License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License
Intended Audience :: Developers
Topic :: Communications :: Email
Topic :: Communications :: Usenet News
Topic :: Internet :: WWW/HTTP
Topic :: Text Processing
```

... and then run `python setup.py --classifiers` to test:

```
Intended Audience :: Developers
Topic :: Text Processing
Development Status :: 3 - Alpha
License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License
Programming Language :: Python :: Implementation :: PyPy
```

That's... not right.  It appears that the configuration parser is treating the first colon in each classifier as an assignment operator (so that `Development Status :: 3 - Alpha` is parsed the same way as `Development = : 3 - Alpha`) and then Setuptools joins the key & value back together with `:`. (This theory is supported by the fact that I get the exact same output if I replace the first colon on each line with `=`.)  Because of this, any classifier with the same initial component as a previous classifier will overwrite the previous one, and so Setuptools will only see the last `Programming Language` line, the last `Topic` line, etc.

It might be possible to fix this by initializing the `configparser` object with `delimiters=('=',), allow_no_value=True`, but this wouldn't work in Python 2, and it'd affect all other config sections as well (but how many people use colons as the delimiter in `setup.cfg`, anyway?).

issue_title: add option for **not** installing dependencies automatically
issue_body:
Right now, setuptools tries to download missing dependencies automatically. This is good in the general case, but for packaging systems/distributions it would be useful to have the option to tell setuptools **not** to install missing dependencies automatically, so the dependencies have to be recorded correctly by the packager in the packaging system.
For this reason, please add a flag that turns off automatic installation of packages.

issue_title: [feature]: Adding support for S3 server-side-encryption using a KMS (SSE-KMS)
issue_body:
This is a feature request for adding SSE-KMS support. For general S3 SSE-KMS reference please take a look at: 
 - [General SSE-KMS overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/en_en/AmazonS3/latest/dev/UsingKMSEncryption.html)
 - [SSE-KMS encryption context](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/encryption-context.html)
- [Go reference implementation](https://github.com/minio/minio-go/blob/master/pkg/encrypt/server-side.go#L85)

The minio-py SDK should provide a similar API as the minio-go (as long as possible by language-dependent / idiomatic code)

Add pagination to the toolbox component
issue_body: Add a pagination functionalities to the the toolbox component.

issue_title: Azure Web App Deployment
issue_body:
I was looking for a simple way to run a Python based serverless function on the web ... and while I did not found what I want, I found that Azure has a close to serverless IIS Web App available

In the back is a full dedicated Windows with IIS, but you deploy it just from Git :)

I ended up creating a repo to document how to run Klein on Azure. https://github.com/adiroiban/azure-web-app-klein

Not sure if it makes sense to have this as part of the Klein documentation... but just in the case in which someone wants to run Klein on Azure, I left this comment here.

issue_title: Parsing error: can not read a block mapping entry
issue_body:
I'm experiencing a weird parsing error. I have the following in a YAML file;
```
/music/{release}:
  title: "· • {{resources.title}} · BLAH BLAH · • ·"
  og:type: music.album
  image: resources.image.source
  canonical: /music/{release}
```
And I'm getting the following error from JS-YAML;
```
JS-YAML: can not read a block mapping entry; a multiline key may not be an implicit key at line 3, column 10:
      og:type: music.album
```
I can't figure out why this is happening. Is my YAML syntax correct? Any other ideas?

issue_title: [writer] Prevent unnecessary space at end of lines with three-space indent
Omelette du fromage
issue_body: *Omelette du fromage* is not a correct way to say *cheese omelet* in french. It means *cheese's omelet*. *Omelette au fromage* is the correct way.

issue_body: Thanks for sharing this code. It was written for Python 2 though, which seems odd in this era.

issue_title: Logging error or endless blocking
issue_body:
Hi there,
First of all I would like to than you for pykafka - it makes my work much easier.
I have a very strange problem, and it appears only on some of the machines I'm using.
So I have a class that handles producer. In I create a producer attribute like this:
```python
self.producer = self.topic.get_sync_producer(max_request_size=max_request_size)
```
Sending data is done by a simple method:
```python
def save(self, data):
    self.producer.produce(data)
```
This producer handler is used in a loop, sending chunks of some data.
Now, the problem is that when there is no data, program freezes with logging message:
`2016-12-06 13:12:22 [DEBUG] "Blocking until all messages are sent"`
I tried to fix this problem by forcing producer (or rather brokers) to shutdown in a destructor method of my handler:
```python
def __del__(self):
    if self.producer._owned_brokers is not None:
            for owned_broker in self.producer._owned_brokers is not None:
                owned_broker.stop()
```
wchich is a code piece copied from pykafka producer finalizing code (https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/master/pykafka/producer.py#L287).
Now for the funny part. This code actually stops my program from freezing, but sometimes (and I don't know why not always) it produces a logging error. The data is already sent, but this makes handling this program much more difficult:
```

issue_title: Add some way to control order of created nodes
issue_body:
There is some preliminary work in #14 for controlling order of nodes created when terms are passed into the constructor, but supporting order there without supporting it more generally seems strange.

I don't think it requires a subclass, should be optional behavior on default XmlObject class.

We'll have to figure out a way to specify order on the class, and then figure out how to access that logic on the field descriptors so node creation can be constrained by the configured order.

issue_title: inspect.getargspec() is deprecated since Python 3.0
issue_body:
I'm getting the following warning running a simple function with the `@log_capture()` decorator with `python3.6`:

```python
/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/testfixtures/utils.py:36: DeprecationWarning: inspect.getargspec() is deprecated, use inspect.signature() or inspect.getfullargspec()
  to_add = len(getargspec(wrapped)[0][len(args):])
```

It seem to be [deprecated since Python 3.0](https://docs.python.org/3/library/inspect.html#inspect.getargspec).

issue_title: Mock Subprocess Context Manager
issue_body:
With a little experimentation I was able to mock a subprocess being used as a context manager. I'd like to open a PR and add an example to the docs but thought I'd put up here first to see if there might be a cleaner way to achieve this?

```python
def my_func():
    with Popen('svn ls -R foo', stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE, shell=True) as proccess:
        out, err = process.communicate()
        return out 
```

```python
def fake_context_enter(mock_instance, *args, **kw):
    return mock_instance

def fake_context_exit(mock_instance, *args, **kw):
    return mock_instance

class TestMyFunc(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.Popen = MockPopen()
        self.Popen.mock.Popen.return_value.__enter__ = fake_context_enter
        self.Popen.mock.Popen.return_value.__exit__ = fake_context_exit
        self.r = Replacer()
        self.r.replace(dotted_path, self.Popen)
        self.addCleanup(self.r.restore)

    def test_example(self):
        # set up
        self.Popen.set_command('svn ls -R foo', stdout=b'o', stderr=b'e')
```

issue_title: Release v2.0
issue_body:
FYI: I've added a `v2.0` milestone and removed the `v1.3` milestone.

My suggestion would be that we release `v1.2` now, and reserve backward-incompatible changes to a `v2.0` release, to be done in ~ 1 month.

There's many issues currently under the `v2.0` milestone, but some of the key proposed changes that would justify a major version bump would be changes that are silently and sometimes slightly backward-incompatible like #89 or #35.

Another goal of 2.0 I think should be to get rid of the bundled Kapteyn like @sargas already attempted in #54 , so that `pyregion` can easily be packaged by Debian and others (they have to remove bundled files, it's not allowed).

@leejjoon , all - Sounds like a plan? Thoughts?

issue_title: PSF photometry on crowded CCD frames
issue_body:
Hi there. I occasionally need to perform PSF photometry on crowded fields, and I'm looking to get rid of IRAF as soon as possible. This package seems to be ideal replacement, but for what I can gather `photutils` it is still in a heavy development phase.

Below are the steps needed to complete a full PSF photometry process, and the packages in IRAF used in each step (as described in [A User's Guide to Stellar CCD Photometry with IRAF](https://www.mn.uio.no/astro/english/services/it/help/visualization/iraf/daophot2.pdf)). I was wondering if these features are in the roadmap to be implemented, or if they are not planned at all.
1. Alignment of CCD images taken with different filters and exposure times (`imalign`)
2. Combine images with equivalent exposures to reduce S/N ratio (`imcombine`)
3. Standard stars aperture photometry (`daophot --> daofind, phot`)
4. Standard stars catalog to convert instrumental magnitudes to standard (`photcal --> mkcatalog, mknobsfile, mkconfig, fitparams`)
5. PSF photometry on all filters and exposures observed for the crowded field (`daophot --> daofind, phot, psf, nstar, substar, allstar`)
6. Transformation of stars' magnitudes in crowded field to standard system (`daophot --> mkobsfile, invertfit`)
7. Combine all exposures into a single observation for all stars in the field (no IRAF task as far as I am aware)

Sorry for the long post.

issue_title: Events not delivered when connection is dropped
issue_body:
# Problem

We have a Fluentd instance running behind an Amazon Load Balancer. Load balancer has a [timeout setting](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/classic/config-idle-timeout.html) (defaults to 60s, for purposes of testing I have set it to 5s). As per docs: "If no data has been sent or received by the time that the idle timeout period elapses, the load balancer closes the connection."

When sending data, the FluentSender tries to reuse the connection if possible, and reconnect in case of a `socket.error`. If we keep sending events one after another (time between sends is < timeout) everything works fine. But if we consider the following example:

* Send event no. 1
* Wait for timeout (e.g. 10s)
* Send event no. 2

Since the connection has been closed by the load balancer, I would expect to receive a socket error when sending event no.2. But the FluentSender (or specifically, the `socket` object) does not seem to register that the connection was dropped. There is no error when sending event no. 2 (`emit` will return `True`), but the event never reaches the destination.

## Debugging info

This was tested on Python 2.7.6 and Python 3.4.3.

I have prepared some examples to demonstrate the issue. For simplicity, we are using the `socket` directly, without using the FluentSender.

### Example 1

In this example we send 1 event, then wait for timeout, and send a few more events. There is no socket error, even though the connection was dropped.

Code:

```python
import socket
import time

sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
sock.settimeout(3.0)
sock.connect(('myhost, 24224))

# send event no. 1
print('Sending event 1')
sock.sendall('event 1')

# wait for timeout
print('Waiting for timeout...')
time.sleep(10)

# send more events
for i in range(2, 10):
    print('Sending event {}'.format(i))
    sock.sendall('event {}'.format(i))
```

Output:

```
Sending event 1
Waiting for timeout...
Sending event 2
Sending event 3
Sending event 4
Sending event 5
Sending event 6
Sending event 7
Sending event 8
Sending event 9
Sending event 10
```

### Example 2


This is a similar example as Example 1, but with a short delay between additional events. In this case we do get a socket error, but only when trying to send the 4th event after the timeout.

Code:

```python
# ... snip

# send more events
for i in range(2, 10):
    print('Sending event {}'.format(i))
    sock.sendall('event {}'.format(i))

    # add a short delay before sending the next event
    time.sleep(0.1)
```

Output:

```
Sending event 1
Waiting for timeout...
Sending event 2
Sending event 3
Sending event 4
Sending event 5
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "socktst.py", line 18, in <module>
    sock.sendall('event {}'.format(i))
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/socket.py", line 224, in meth
    return getattr(self._sock,name)(*args)
socket.error: [Errno 32] Broken pipe
```

# Solution?

One way of solving this would be to add an option to always reconnect (False by default), instead of reusing the connection. The problem might also be caused with the way the load balancer drops connections, but not sure how to check this. So any help on this is appreciated.

issue_title: Simplify travis parameters
issue_body:
## Description
Currently we have a few travis parameters that pertain to handling how the tests/documentation behaves for different configurations.

These include:
  - `MINIMUM_REQUIREMENTS`
  - `OPTIONAL_DEPS`
  - `BUILD_DOCS`

I think the only necessary one is `MINIMUM_REQUIREMENTS`. The others were necessary for 2.7/3 compatibility. 

I think we should cleanup the logic such that 
  - `OPTIONAL_DEPS = not MINIMUM_REQUIREMENTS`

issue_title: Remove stripUnlearnedColumns from compute in SPRegion
issue_body:
Finishes replacing outdated issue #1262 (followup to issue #2278)

```stripUnlearnedColumns``` does not need to be default behavior within compute. This will only affect the case of a spacial pooler region being run with learning mode disabled AFTER learning has occurred. 

After ```stripUnlearnedColumns``` is removed, the default behavior for this case will be for active, unlearned columns to remain in the output of the spacial pooler if learning is disabled. This does not affect the spacial pooler if learning is enabled or if it is being with only it's randomly initialized values (no learning enabled).

issue_title: Inconsistent ScalarEncoder constructor behavior
issue_body:
Buried in `ScalarEncoder._initEncoder()`, there is the possibility for raising a `ValueError` exception suggesting that one of `n`, `radius`, or `resolution` is required, but it seems `n` is always required.  For example, if you pass `n=0`, and specify either `radius` or `resolution` to something non-zero, it raises an exception: `AttributeError: 'ScalarEncoder' object has no attribute 'rangeInternal'`

This passes, resulting in a successfully created `ScalarEncoder` instance:

```python
encoder = ScalarEncoder(3, None, None, name='scalar', n=14, radius=0, resolution=0, forced=True)
```

These fail, raising unexpected `AttributeError` exceptions:

```python
encoder1 = ScalarEncoder(3, None, None, name='scalar', n=0, radius=None, resolution=0.5, forced=True)
encoder2 = ScalarEncoder(3, None, None, name='scalar', n=0, radius=1.5, resolution=0, forced=True)
```

issue_title: AppVeyor creates a Debug C++ library
Speed up travis build time
issue_body:
I've noticed that `nupic.core` currently uses single-thread make on travis, and did some speed up experiments.

Under Linux, we could use `make -j$(nproc)` to tell make to use as many threads as the cpu cores.

Under Mac, however, no such utility could be found(by me), but it's easy to alias one: `alias nproc="sysctl -n hw.logicalcpu"`.

My experiment speeds up the compilation on Travis by almost half:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/64258/14946803/10fa0ca4-105b-11e6-9b17-e30c6c1203cf.png)

My work on #324 is a little bit stuck, so I opened this issue to add this sooner, so we could wait on Travis less. 😉

issue_title: Add a settings property on backends
issue_body:
When a backend is instanciated, it gets some settings and creates a connection object with it. In some our tests we're checking the values of those settings to check that they are correctly passed along.

I propose a simple standard for all our backends to expose those settings: a "settings" read-only property that will let us read the values.

Implementation example for the redis backends:

```python

class RedisBackend:
    @property
    def settings(self):
        data = dict(self._client.connection_pool.connection_kwargs)
        data['max_connections'] = self._client.connection_pool.max_connections
        return data
```

issue_title: RFC importing unicode_literals in each module that formats literal strings
issue_body:
Relevant Zenodo Issue: https://github.com/zenodo/zenodo/issues/1305

Should we establish a practice that all ``from __future__`` imports also import ``unicode_literals``?
On Zenodo we are having some of those production errors, and to fully solve this problem it requires uniform handling of string literals formatting in all of invenio.

This would require a rather straightforward grep+replace script and PR campaign across inveniosoftware, as well as changes to cookiecutter and maybe ``./run-tests.sh`` to run this flake8 module: https://github.com/cogniteev/flake8-unicode-string-literal (although if we enforce it everywhere, it should not raise any warnings).

issue_title: ffpyplayer can't be built with new toolchain
issue_body:
`ffpyplayer` and it's dependency `ffmpeg` have no recipe for new toolchain (while both of them work in old toolchain). Current `ffmpeg` recipe [is broken and has nothing to do](https://github.com/kivy/python-for-android/issues/920#issuecomment-265135608) with one ffpyplayer needs.

New toolchain can be used with `sdl2` recipe only, that's mean we should use `ffpyplayer==4.0.0` and `sdl2` as dependency for it (while old toolchain uses `ffpyplayer==3.2` and `sdl`). I tried to create recipe for v4.0.0, but got no success.

Since `ffpyplayer` is probably [only way](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/kivy-users/ypJOZXlUEqM) to support video on Android devices it makes impossible to use video in Kivy on Android using new toolchain.

Btw, there's no docs about video in Kivy on Android it makes almost impossible for new users to understand what happening.

issue_title: pandas_datareader/yahoo/daily.py should be named differently
issue_body:
Hello,

`pandas_datareader/google/daily.py` provide only daily data

but

`pandas_datareader/yahoo/daily.py` use `interval` so it might provide
not only daily data.

    interval : string, default 'd'
        Time interval code, valid values are 'd' for daily, 'w' for weekly,
        'm' for monthly and 'v' for dividend.

So maybe we should name it differently.

Kind regards

issue_title: change link
issue_body:
https://github.com/pydata/pandas-datareader/blob/dcc09eb0c59a91094499b0f12a63584638c5bd94/pandas_datareader/google/daily.py#L47

The line above should be changed to:
return 'https://finance.google.com/finance/historical'

to be able to download historical data.

issue_title: Decide on the package name
issue_body:
Currently, @hayd changed the package name to `pandas_datareader`. Is everybody satisfied with that? (to be clear: it is about the name that is `import`ed)

Personally, I find it a bit long, and I think I also don't really like the underscore in the name. But of course, it is just personal taste! (I justed wanted to bring up the discussion to have this now, and not later, and deliberately decide on this to keep it as it is or to change it). 
What about just `datareader`, or `pddatareader` (but that is a bit difficult with the two 'd's)

issue_title: Please promote awesome password standards by creating fa-secure-passwd
issue_body:
(cross-posted from the [FAPro ticket](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome-Pro/issues/334).)

The developer community needs a push toward doing a better job with passwords.

We can't go more than a few months without hearing about a huge [data breach](https://www.troyhunt.com/observations-and-thoughts-on-the-linkedin-data-breach/) where unsalted password hashes are sold.

More troubling, I recently clicked "forgot password" on a website. The company sent my password to me via email. Plaintext. Thus, they are storing plaintext passwords in their database, begging to be hacked. I still don't know what to think about companies that won't accept special characters. For instance, my bank won't let me use my "tier 1" password. They say: "You can't use $ in a password." What are they doing to cause that constraint? Doesn't seem good.

So my suggestion / plea to @FortAwesome is to come up with a symbol that means "we promise not to store plaintext passwords. In fact we use one of the Fort-Awesome-password-committee-approved one-directional salted hash methods." People could use that icon as a symbol to be trusted where they collected passwords. Then I would know when I could trust a site with one of the passwords I use broadly.

Something like:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/32432/24373210/4297f6e2-12f6-11e7-81e4-e5b26c441128.png)

maybe a salt bottle with a padlock or ... you're designers, come up with something. :)

Even if you had some "false-positives"*, you would still be doing a great service to the dev community by promoting high standards and best practices. 

I suspect that, given your license, you would have to work some serious magic to get this to be at all viable. Still, it's a noble goal. Go forth and awesome, please.

* where false-positives is a euphemism for people using it even though they hadn't implemented those standards...

issue_title: Cannot use both regular and solid fonts together in the same Qt/QML application
issue_body:
I often use Font Awesome in Qt/QML applications. In v4, this was quite straightforward, all that needed to be done was using a [FontLoader](http://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qml-qtquick-fontloader.html) and then set the [font.family](http://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qml-qtquick-text.html#font.family-prop) attribute of text elements to the font family (which could be retrieved via the `FontLoader`).

With v5, we now have several font files (e.g. there are 3 `*.ttf` files in the Free version). Generally, it is possible to use multiple `FontLoader`s to load all of them. However, I noticed that the font family for the regular and solid variants are the same. Hence, I cannot easily switch between the two by setting the `font.family` attribute of text objects.

I would have expected to be able to select the variant by one of the following:

* Setting the [font.weight](http://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qml-qtquick-text.html#font.weight-prop) property. This did not work out for some reasons - maybe a Qt issue?
* Setting the [font.styleName](http://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qml-qtquick-text.html#font.styleName-prop) property, i.e. `Solid` vs `Regular`. However, this also did not work (because all the font files are set to `Regular`).

For the time being, I worked around the issue by editing the file `fa-solid-900.ttf` with [FontForge](https://fontforge.github.io) and setting the font family to *Font Awesome 5 Free Solid*. With the modified `*.ttf` file I can easily switch between the two styles in my QML application. It would however be cool if Font Awesome would work out of the box with Qt/QML ;-)

Thanks for considering!

Btw: The brands font file seems to work just fine, however, this is probably due to it uses a different font family name compared to the other two.

issue_title: Icon request: Fishing Rod and Fish
issue_body:
### What can it be used for?

The concept of Fishing [can be dated back to 40,000 years ago](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_fishing). I was somewhat shocked to see there isn't an icon for the Angling or Fishing communities at all! (Yes, there's a difference!). There are many use cases for such an icon, Taken together, commercial and recreational fishing contributed nearly $90 billion to the U.S. economy in 2012, according to Hamilton Project data, including over 1.5 million jobs for American workers, and since then it's been on the increase. The UK government (my country) [reports that](https://www.gov.uk/government/news/fishing-industry-in-2016-statistics-published) UK vessels landed 701 thousand tonnes of sea fish into the UK and abroad with a value of £936 million. Whether it be discussing Angling & Fishing on social media to icons for the businesses specialising in the industry, there is a lot of potential for usage.  A fish caught on a rod, and a fish not on a rod (both of which FA doesn't have) would be tremendously useful. 

### Example image (optional)

I think we all know what a fish or a fishing rod looks like

### Icon request checklist

- [x ] This is a single icon or matched pair (Ex: `lock` / `unlock`)
- [ x] The title starts with "Icon request: " and is followed by the requested icon name (Ex: `Icon request: magical-unicorn`)
- [x ] I have [searched for existing issues](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/issues) and to the best of my knowledge this is not a duplicate
- [ ] I have included at least one example image if it could be helpful (optional)
- [x ] The request is for a concrete object (it's harder to make an icon to represent happiness, it's easier to make a smiley face. ☺)

issue_title: unicode(v) doesn't work on python 3+
issue_body:
When iterating over `data_dict` if there is a file to upload, there is a call to `unicode` that breaks on Python 3.

```python
            if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
                v = unicode(v)
            data_dict[k.encode('utf-8')] = v.encode('utf-8')
```
https://github.com/ckan/ckanapi/blob/master/ckanapi/common.py#L80-L82

adding

`from builtins import str as text` 

and changing to 

```python
            if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
                v = text(v)
            data_dict[k.encode('utf-8')] = v.encode('utf-8')
```

Seems to resolve the issue. PR forthcoming.

issue_title: for use in python and ipython repls, check if IPython hasattr display
Named maps handling
issue_body:
I see that named maps are used to show the embedded maps.  I'd rather see us using [anonymous maps](https://carto.com/docs/carto-engine/maps-api/anonymous-maps/).

The advantage to this, is that we don't create named maps, which is a limited resource for some users with a lot of data (since there is one named map per dataset and per map). The limitation of using anonymous maps, is that they are only valid for two hours, after which, you have to regenerate them. But I think that fits pretty well for the cartoframes usage.

cc @andy-esch @stuartlynn

issue_title: Expand worklogs if total > maxResults
issue_body:
Currently, if a JIRA Issue has worklogs, that field response is structured as follows:

```
{
    "expand": "renderedFields,names,schema,operations,editmeta,changelog,versionedRepresentations",
    "id": "316785",
    "self": "https://server.jirainstance.com/rest/api/2/issue/316785",
    "key": "ABC-123",
    "fields": {
        ...
        "worklog": {
            "startAt": 0,
            "maxResults": 20,
            "total": 2454,
            "worklogs": [{wl1}, {wl2}...{wl20},]
            }
        }
    }
```

Presently, whether you use the JIRA.issue(key) method or the jira.search_issues(key, expand='worklog') method, the API call only returns the first 20 worklogs. It appears to be impossible to get more than 20 worklogs. 

However, if you hit https://server.jirainstance.com/rest/api/2/issue/316785/worklog, this will return all worklogs as a JSON, where maxResults == total. 

We have reporting that depends on using these worklogs, but we're currently impeded by this limitation. Can we possibly use this issue/123456/worklog API call in order to supply JIRA.issue.fields.worklog with data, rather than relying on the data from the issue/123456 API call itself? I'm happy to take a stab at a PR.

Open to other approaches to solving this problem. Is it possible to increase maxResults using the jira.issue() or any other method? 

Thanks!

issue_title: Cannot run chalice deploy with '--stage' parameter when using @app.on_s3_event
issue_body:
This one is fairly obvious. 

Using chalice for s3 event triggers means you must have a pre-existing s3 bucket. When deploying with the stage parameter this creates you a new role and lambda but still the s3 bucket must pre-exist.

Because of this the behavior in s3 remains the same as a normal `chalice deploy`. Chalice will put an event on the s3 bucket to point to the new lambda.

So if I run `chalice deploy` and then `chalice deploy --stage test` I get this:
```
(env) GC02RK59EG8WPE:photo_analytics trentonmcfarlane$ chalice deploy --stage test
Creating deployment package.
Updating lambda function: photo_analytics-test-handler
Configuring S3 events in bucket photo-bucket-trent to function photo_analytics-test-handler
Traceback (most recent call last):

issue_title: Destination of containers when using harness.py
issue_body:
Hi I'm using harness.py to package up protocols to use on Transcriptic.

When a user specifies a container in the dialogue window, where the container is used in the protocol like so:

```python
# protocol.py
solid_culture_plate = params.solid_culture_plate
```
```json
# manifest.json

"inputs": {
        "solid_culture_plate": {
          "type": "container",
          "description": "Choose the container to plate the bacteria into"
        }
}
```
Where is the storage location specified for this container in the protocol?

I think it might be in harness.py:
```python
class ProtocolInfo(object):
    def __init__(self, json):
        self.input_types = json['inputs']

    def parse(self, protocol, inputs):
        refs = inputs['refs']
        params = inputs['parameters']

        for name in refs:
            ref = refs[name]
            c = protocol.ref(
                name,
                ref.get('id'),
                ref['type'],
                storage=ref.get('store'),
                discard=ref.get('discard'))
            aqs = ref.get('aliquots')
            if aqs:
                for idx in aqs:
                    aq = aqs[idx]
                    c.well(idx).set_volume(aq['volume'])
                    if "properties" in aq:
                        c.well(idx).set_properties(aq.get('properties'))

        out_params = {}
        for k in self.input_types:
            typeDesc = self.input_types[k]
            out_params[k] = convert_param(protocol, params.get(k), typeDesc)

        return out_params
```
But I could be wrong. Maybe ```storage=ref.get('store')```?

The problem I have is that I am running protocols with samples originally stored at ```cold_4``` but it is storing them at ```ambient``` after the protocol has executed and I need to fix this.

Thanks for your help, apologies in advance if I missed something, I tried to learn what I could from the source.

issue_title: What is the correct way in proto3 to use HasField?
issue_body:
Originally came up in #1329. `HasField` with `proto3` fails on simple / singular / non-message fields.

@nathanielmanistaatgoogle Mentioned that @haberman might be able to shine some light. Josh, what should we be doing here? The [current approach][1] isn't great:

```python
    # NOTE: As of proto3, HasField() only works for message fields, not for
    #       singular (non-message) fields. First try to use HasField and
    #       if it fails (with a ValueError) we manually consult the fields.
    try:
        return message_pb.HasField(property_name)
    except ValueError:
        all_fields = set([field.name for field in message_pb._fields])
        return property_name in all_fields
```

-----

Should we bother with [checking "has field"][2], e.g.

```python
if _has_field(key_pb.partition_id, 'dataset_id'):
    ...
```

or should we instead just check Truth-iness

```python
if key_pb.partition_id.dataset_id:
    ...
# OR MORE STRICT
if key_pb.partition_id.dataset_id != '':
    ...
```


[1]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/896044579b5dfaddb75e8c7cbf72b614b0d04c5e/gcloud/_helpers.py#L403-L410
[2]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/896044579b5dfaddb75e8c7cbf72b614b0d04c5e/gcloud/datastore/connection.py#L405

Hi,

  I had some python scripts using this library to do requests on BigQuery but  after updating google-cloud-bigquery to version 0.26 I am having some issues executing these scripts.

  I am currently running Fedora 25, with python 2.7 and google.cloud.bigquery is on version 0.26.0, my script looks like this at the moment:

```
    client = bigquery.Client(project='myprojectid')

    q = """
           SELECT co.w, 
                  co.n n_now, 
                  po.n n_prv,

        LEFT JOIN trends.PreviousYearsOccurences_all po

              AND co.n > 5

    while(d.state != "DONE"):

issue_title: Pub/Sub subscriber flow control stops ack requests
issue_body:
Pub/Sub client library stops sending Ack or modifyAckDeadline requests entirely when flow control settings are defined.  See code below: when ran as it is against a subscription with a large backlog, it shows that the client thinks it's processing ~700 messages/second, while there is no outgoing traffic from the machine and the monitoring metrics show no ack | modAckDeadline traffic.

If we comment out the flow control argument, the overall processing rate is the same, acks and modifyAckDeadlines are regularly and successfully received by the server.

Expected behavior: flow control settings do not prevent the client library from acknowledging the message.

1. OS type and version
Mac Os Sierra
2. Python version and virtual environment information `python --version`
2.7
3. google-cloud-python version `pip show google-cloud`, `pip show google-<service>` or `pip freeze`
 pip show google-cloud-pubsub
Name: google-cloud-pubsub
Version: 0.28.4
4. Stacktrace if available
5. Steps to reproduce
- Run without flow control setting against a subscription with a large (10K messages) backlog
- Run without flow control setting against a subscription with a large (10K messages) backlog
6. Code example
```python
import datetime
import sys
import time
import threading

from google.cloud import pubsub_v1 as pubsub

subscription_name = "projects/%s/subscriptions/%s"%(sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2])
sleep_time_ms = 0
try:
    sleep_time_ms = int(sys.argv[3])
except Exception:
    print "Could not parse custom sleep time."

print "Using sleep time %g ms"%sleep_time_ms
start_time = -1
messages_processed = 0.0
thread_lock = threading.Lock()


def callback(message):
    global start_time, thread_lock,messages_processed

    if start_time < 0 :
        with thread_lock: start_time = time.time()

    time.sleep(float(sleep_time_ms)/1000)
    message.ack()
    with thread_lock:
        messages_processed += 1


subscription = pubsub.SubscriberClient().subscribe(subscription_name, flow_control = pubsub.types.FlowControl(max_messages=100)).open(callback=callback)

while True:
    time.sleep(5)
    print "Acking messages at rate %.5f / second"%( messages_processed / (time.time() - start_time))
```

Using GitHub flavored markdown can help make your request clearer.
See: https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/

issue_title: accounts_manager is overwriting existing linux account
issue_body:
We are running some legacy software on Google Compute, that creates a well known linux user account, with a home directory of `/export/home/<user>` and set some specific sudo rules.
If we use gcloud to SSH into a box as that same user, the Google daemon overwrites the users passwd entry and breaks the sudo rules.
It would helpful if either:
- the google daemon detected the existing user settings and didn't trash them
- we are able to blacklist certain usernames that we don't want the google daemon to manage

issue_body:
I recently started using dill instead of pickle in my project and it's really great. However I ran into some problem. I have the following code:

test.py:
```python
import dill

class Meta(type): pass

class A(object):
    __metaclass__ = Meta

b = A()
with open('somefile', 'wb') as f:
    dill.dump(b,f)
```

Now I execute it with `python2 test.py`, it creates `somefile` and everything is fine. But when I open the interpreter and try `b = dill.load(open('somefile'))` I get the following error:

    AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
    <ipython-input-1-4fc5ba18542c> in <module>()
    ----> 1 j = pickle.load(open('somefile'))

    /usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dill-0.2.2-py2.7.egg/dill/dill.pyc in load(file)
        197     pik = Unpickler(file)

    --> 199     obj = pik.load()
        200     if type(obj).__module__ == _main_module.__name__: # point obj class to main
        201         try: obj.__class__ == getattr(pik._main_module, type(obj).__name__)

    /usr/lib/python2.7/pickle.pyc in load(self)
        856             while 1:
        857                 key = read(1)
    --> 858                 dispatch[key](self)
        859         except _Stop, stopinst:
        860             return stopinst.value

    /usr/lib/python2.7/pickle.pyc in load_global(self)
       1088         module = self.readline()[:-1]
       1089         name = self.readline()[:-1]
    -> 1090         klass = self.find_class(module, name)
       1091         self.append(klass)

    /usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dill-0.2.2-py2.7.egg/dill/dill.pyc in find_class(self, module, name)
        276         if (module, name) == ('__builtin__', '__main__'):
        277             return self._main_module.__dict__ #XXX: above set w/save_module_dict
    --> 278         return StockUnpickler.find_class(self, module, name)
        279     pass

If I remove `__metaclass__` from class definition, everything works fine. issue_title: tox fails with SyntaxError when wrong version of dependency is used
issue_body: `fox-2.7` will fail with a `SyntaxError` if pointed to dependencies built with `3.x`, and `fox-3.5` will fail similarly if pointed to dependencies built with `2.x`.  This is due to `multiprocess` and `ppft` having separate code for `2.x` and `3.x`.

issue_title: Condition referencing unknown variable condition when serializing
issue_body:
When serializing to JSON, Condition of the aws module references a condition instance variable of ConditionElement

```python
def JSONrepr(self):
    d = {}
    for c in self.conditions:
        d[c.condition] = c.get_dict()
```

However, ConditionElement has instance variables of key, value, and cond_dict.  This situation causes an issue when serializing

```bash
...
File "/projects/aws/python-3.4/lib64/python3.4/site-packages/awacs/aws.py", line 106, in JSONrepr
    d[c.condition] = c.get_dict()
AttributeError: 'ConditionElement' object has no attribute 'condition'
```

The Condition class utilizes a list of ConditionElement instances.  These instances could be serialized provided the ConditionElement also provides serialization.

The title specified in troposphere is not the actual physical resource name and should be validated as such (https://github.com/cloudtools/troposphere/blob/fe72f7d3f7b0711a22173c1240134173aafef574/troposphere/iam.py#L75-L77).

The next snippet was created today using `troposphere==1.5.0`and `boto3==1.2.6`:

```python
>>> import boto3
>>> import troposphere
>>> import troposphere.iam
>>> import time
>>>
>>> long_title = 'VeryLongName{}'.format('Z' * 100)
>>> print len(long_title)
112
>>>
>>> # create a role
...
>>> role = troposphere.iam.Role(
...     long_title,
...     AssumeRolePolicyDocument={
...         "Statement": [{
...             "Action": ["sts:AssumeRole"],
...             "Effect": "Allow",
...             "Principal": {"Service": ["ec2.amazonaws.com"]}
...         }]
...     })
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 8, in <module>
  File "/Users/hugo/.virtualenvs/tmp-5ce4367de56b6bde/lib/python2.7/site-packages/troposphere/__init__.py", line 44, in __init__
    self.validate_title()
  File "/Users/hugo/.virtualenvs/tmp-5ce4367de56b6bde/lib/python2.7/site-packages/troposphere/iam.py", line 77, in validate_title
    iam_role_name(self.title)
  File "/Users/hugo/.virtualenvs/tmp-5ce4367de56b6bde/lib/python2.7/site-packages/troposphere/validators.py", line 98, in iam_role_name
    raise ValueError('IAM Role Name may not exceed 64 characters')
ValueError: IAM Role Name may not exceed 64 characters
>>>
>>>
>>> # delete validator
...
>>> del troposphere.iam.Role.validate_title
>>> # try again
...
>>> role = troposphere.iam.Role(
...     long_title,
...     AssumeRolePolicyDocument={
...         "Statement": [{
...             "Action": ["sts:AssumeRole"],
...             "Effect": "Allow",
...             "Principal": {"Service": ["ec2.amazonaws.com"]}
...         }]
...     })
>>> template = troposphere.Template()
>>> template.add_resource(role)
<troposphere.iam.Role object at 0x10ee02990>
>>> print template.to_json()
{
    "Resources": {
        "VeryLongNameZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ": {
            "Properties": {
                "AssumeRolePolicyDocument": {
                    "Statement": [
                        {
                            "Action": [
                                "sts:AssumeRole"
                            ],
                            "Effect": "Allow",
                            "Principal": {
                                "Service": [
                                    "ec2.amazonaws.com"
                                ]
                            }
                        }
                    ]
                }
            },
            "Type": "AWS::IAM::Role"
        }
    }
}
>>> client = boto3.client('cloudformation', 'us-east-1')
>>> stack = client.create_stack(
...     StackName='testTroposphere',
...     TemplateBody=template.to_json(),
...     Capabilities=['CAPABILITY_IAM'])
>>>
>>> while client.describe_stacks(StackName=stack['StackId'])['Stacks'][0]['StackStatus'] != 'CREATE_COMPLETE':
...     import time
...     time.sleep(1)
...
>>> resources = client.describe_stack_resources(StackName=stack['StackId'])
>>> for r in resources['StackResources']:
...     physical_id = r['PhysicalResourceId']
...     print("{} ({} chars)".format(physical_id, len(physical_id)))
...
testTroposphere-VeryLongNameZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ-PTHEM9FPNX28 (63 chars)
```

The snippet above shows that the physical id was chosen by CloudFormation and isn't just a trimmed version of the title (it includes a random part too).

issue_title: pex -e should support console_scripts pointers
issue_body:
pex -e should be changed to use console_scripts entry points.  this will allow for pex -e pex -r pex, where pex = pex.bin.pex:main [whl], hence picking up the whl extra_requires.

pex -m should be added to supplant the prior -e functionality
(do this on a 1.0 since this is a major user-facing compatibility break)

issue_title: daemon state invalidation strategy for e.g. pants.ini modification
issue_body:
currently, modification to `pants.ini` in a given repo doesn't end up invalidating anything in the daemon. if pertinent new configuration is added (e.g. new ignore paths), stale data (e.g. errors in previously unignored paths) can be cached and served by the daemon.

we should probably implement specialized handling for modifications to pants.ini to improve this situation. the same might also be true for `PANTS_*` environment variables.

issue_title: Create `Tasks` struct before constructing the `Scheduler`
issue_body:
Because the `Tasks` struct is currently created post `scheduler_create`, it's necessary to mutate the content of the `Arc` that is holding it. That opens up the possibility of a panic, and makes initialization of the `RuleGraph` awkward (as demonstrated on #4371).

Instead, the `Tasks` struct should be constructed independently before the `Scheduler`, and then passed in, so that the `Scheduler` can hold an immutable reference without the `Arc`-mutation loophole.

issue_title: Package is not translateable by ChainedTranslator(...)
issue_body:
I'm getting a persistent error when trying to build a simple Python program that relies on a particular PyPi package ([`hbmqtt`](https://github.com/beerfactory/hbmqtt))... (Are there restrictions on what packages are supported?)

The error is:

```python
Exception caught: (<class 'pex.resolver.Untranslateable'>)

Exception message: Package SourcePackage(u'file:///Users/josh/Desktop/wah/.pants.d/python-setup/resolved_requirements/CPython-2.7.10/hbmqtt-0.9.1.tar.gz') is not translateable by ChainedTranslator(WheelTranslator, EggTranslator, SourceTranslator)
```
This is on Mac OSX High Sierra with Anaconda Python 3, but I've had the same problem on Ubuntu 16.04 with non-Anaconda Python 2.7. [Debug logs here.](https://gist.github.com/joshburkart/780a18cf09c916356f7f4817bab59842)

I think I've reduced it down to running `./pants test blah:whatev` with the following simple directory tree:
```
.
├── blah
│   ├── BUILD
│   ├── requirements.txt
│   └── whatev.py
├── pants
└── pants.ini
```

BUILD:
```python
python_requirements()

python_tests(
    name="whatev",
    sources=["whatev.py"],
    dependencies=[":hbmqtt"]
)
```

requirements.txt (`hbmqtt` [is a pure-Python package](https://github.com/beerfactory/hbmqtt) -- other packages e.g. `numpy` seem to work fine though):
```
hbmqtt
```

whatev.py:
```python
import hbmqtt
```

pants.ini:
```
[GLOBAL]
pants_version: 1.4.0rc1
# Same error with 1.5.0dev0 & 1.5.0dev2
```

issue_title: usability issues with --changed-parent=HEAD
issue_body:
In some cases I'd like to be able to make a bunch of changes in my repo, then compile just the targets that are changed.  I've been playing with --changed-parent=HEAD and while it works in some cases, it gives errors in other cases that prevent it from being effective.

For example, if I create a new file in a new directory (with no BUILD file), I get an error:

    mkdir bin
    echo "hi" > bin/README.txt
    ./pants --changed-parent=HEAD list 
    Exception caught: (<class 'pants.engine.mapper.ResolveError'>)

    Exception message: Path "bin" contains no BUILD files.

If I delete a directory with a BUILD file, I also get an error that the BUILD file doesn't exist (it doesn't anymore).

If I change a source file that is referenced by an existing BUILD file, it works as expected (listing/compiling the target affected).

I would expect if I delete a directory/file, or modify/add a file somewhere that's not referenced by a pants build, pants would just ignore these changes and list/compile any remaining targets that are modified.

issue_title: Pants Contributor Guide doesn't describe PR/RB duality well
issue_body:
The Pants Contributor Guide doesn't discuss how the use of both PRs and RBs should be handled by a non-committer contributor.

This led to me saying I had a green build, expecting there would be a final round of review, but Stu thought I meant the code on the RB was ready for submission (which it was not, because it lagged the PR).

It would be good if the contributor guide established how to manage this duality, especially in the non-committer case where interpersonal communication is required and can be ambiguous.

issue_title: [pantsd] more graceful failure mode for watchman crash
issue_body:
currently, when a synchronous pailgun request is mid-flight in the daemon it's possible for the daemon to tear itself down (e.g. if watchman crashes or gets killed), which terminates the daemon-side socket, which leads to the following failure mode:

```

issue_title: How to create a wheel file?
issue_body:
Hi all,

I'm trying to figure out how to create a wheel file based on the code. I already have a target that creates a `tar.gz` file:

```python
# Generate an sdist
# has to be separate from the actual target that collects source files (in this case :src)
# because otherwise it'll error out with a cryptic exception
python_library(
  name="sdist",
  dependencies=[
    ":src",
  ],
  provides=setup_py(
    name="barbequeue",
  ...
  )
)
```

and then running `/pants setup-py :sdist` creates a source dist inside the `dist` folder.

Now I'm trying to create a `whl` file, and looks like the option `--setup-py-run` is the ticket. Unfortunately, running:
```bash
./pants setup-py --setup-py-run="bdist_wheel" :sdist
```

returns an error:
```
arbequeue/dist/barbequeue-0.0.1**** Failed to install barbequeue-0.0.1 (caused by: NonZeroExit("received exit code 1 during execution of `[u'/usr/bin/python', '-', 'bdist_wheel']` while trying to execute `[u'/usr/bin/python', '-', 'bdist_wheel']`",)
):
stdout:

stderr:
usage: setup.py [global_opts] cmd1 [cmd1_opts] [cmd2 [cmd2_opts] ...]
   or: setup.py --help [cmd1 cmd2 ...]
   or: setup.py --help-commands
   or: setup.py cmd --help

error: invalid command 'bdist_wheel'
```

I've tried to make the `wheel` package a dependency of the `:sdist` target, to no avail. Anything I can do here? Do I need to add another dependency, or mess with the current `setuptools`?

issue_title: Critical JWT vulnerability
issue_body:
There is a critical vulnerability in bunch of JWT implementation that recently discovered.
See article here:
https://auth0.com/blog/2015/03/31/critical-vulnerabilities-in-json-web-token-libraries/
jose library is vulnerable as well. Here the example of implementation of the approach from article using jose library:
```python
from Crypto.PublicKey import RSA
from json import loads as json_decode, dumps as json_encode

import jose


private_key = RSA.generate(1024)
public_key = private_key.publickey()


def sign_token(claim, key, alg='RS256'):
    return jose.sign(claim, {'k': key}, alg=alg)

def verify_token(jws, key):
    return jose.verify(jws, {'k': key})


if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Token issuer
    claim = {'resource': 'value'}
    jws = sign_token(claim, private_key.exportKey()) # Signed using RSA alg

    # serialization, transfer ....

    # Token forger
    forged_claim = json_decode(jose.b64decode_url(jws.payload))
    forged_claim['resource'] = 'hacked_value'
    # signed using HMAC algorithm with RSA public key
    forged_jws = sign_token(forged_claim, public_key.exportKey(), alg='HS256')

    # Token verification
    print 'verifying valid token:'
    try:
        verify_token(jws, public_key.exportKey())
        print 'success'
    except jose.Error:
        'failed'
    print 'verifying forged token:'
    try:
        verify_token(forged_jws, public_key.exportKey())
        print 'success'
    except jose.Error:
        'failed'
```
The result of execution is following:
```
verifying valid token:
success
verifying forged token:
success
```
Which means that anybody with public key can forge tokens that will pass the verification.

issue_title: Slow S3 upload
issue_body:
Hi.
Wal-e 0.8.1 (python 2.7)
We have PostgreSQL master-slave replication + daily backups over S3, Servers are placed on https://selectel.ru/ virtual server. Two weeks from now, we had to switch master and slave. And after that we have problems with replication and backuping. In case of slow replication (it works, renames files from ready to done in pg_xlog/archive_status) pg_xlog is permanently growing on new master.
We investigated the issue:
1) Old master logs contain stable rate ~ 1500 KiB/s. Current logs have "floating" rate from 10 KiB/s to 2182 KiB/s. It's much more often 20...
2) We tested the network, pinging AWS bucket domain (stable ~140 ms) and testing the channel (speedtest shows 50 Mb/s of upload)
3) We tried to determine, if it's an issue of selectel, amazon lags or somewhat. So we wrote a script, that uploaded file (9Mb) to server and measured upload time. On local machine it takes 10-14 seconds in empty bucket, 400 seconds in backup/wal-push bucket. So do on database server.
4) So we tried changing wal destination to new bucket and... Nothing happend. Speed was still near 20 KiB/s.
5) Next we thought the problem is in uploading multiple files in multiple threads and without any delays between upload (AWS limit? No clue was found in docs). The script was rewritten, and really the time grows, if thread number or iteration number grows. Plus to this, on 3d iteration I've got "Your socket connection to the server was not read from or written to within the timeout period. Idle connections will be closed." Error from AWS.
6) So we tried to reduce pool-size of wal-e from default to 1. Nothing changed, 20 KiB. Than to 16 (from your docs "This can increase throughput significantly". Speed grew up to 1700 KiB for one iteration, but then fell back to 20 KiB.
Now we are stuck:
1) The problem in wal-e reopenning connections?
2) The problem in amazon (some limits)?
3) Some preferences of postgres/wal-e stack (checked, everything is similar)
We will be greatfull for any suggestions.

issue_title: PDFs go blank in fullscreen mode when using the 'escape' key
issue_body:
<!--
Instructions:
 * Fill out the sections below, replace …'s with information about your issue
 * Use the 'preview' function above this text box to verify formatting before submitting
-->

### Observed behavior
I used the escape key to get out of the full screen PDF view.  When I made the PDF full screen again, it was blank.  I repeated this over and over again and found that exactly half of the time, the PDF was missing.  When I used the button to get out of full screen, the PDF showed every time.

Here's a video:
[Screencast from 05-10-2018 05:14:58 PM.zip](https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/files/1993415/Screencast.from.05-10-2018.05.14.58.PM.zip)

### Expected behavior
I expected the PDF to show consistently when moving in and out of fullscreen mode.

### User-facing consequences
Learners would not see their homework.  They could then use that as an excuse to not finish their homework.  They could say "I clicked my homework but I couldn't see it!"  That's a dire consequence.

### Errors and logs
There was nothing relevant in the console.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Login to Kolibri.  I was logged in as a coach.
2. Go to 'Learn' mode and find some PDF content in one of the channels.
3. Click the fullscreen icon
4. Press `escape` on the keyboard
5. Click the fullscreen icon again
6. If you discovered the bug, then at this point the PDF will not show.
7+ Repeat this many times.  You will find that every second time, the PDF doesn't show.

### Context
 - Kolibri version: *Kolibri 0.10.0.dev.3*
 - Operating system: *Arch Linux*
 - Browser: *66.0.3359.139 (Official Build) Arch Linux (64-bit)*

issue_title: Don't set dirty bit when only updating MPTT fields (which aren't serialized)
issue_body:
By default, when we do a save/update on a syncable model, we set the dirty bit. When a node in an MPTT tree is moved, django-mptt often [updates the MPTT fields (left/right/level/tree_id)](https://github.com/django-mptt/django-mptt/blob/8bcbe20984fcdcb85dabb6588bf0c423ff1209bf/mptt/managers.py#L690) for a number of other nodes as well. Since we'll be excluding these MPTT fields from serialization/syncing, there's no need to set the dirty bit.

The easiest way to do this looks like it would be overriding [`_mptt_update`](https://github.com/django-mptt/django-mptt/blob/8bcbe20984fcdcb85dabb6588bf0c423ff1209bf/mptt/managers.py#L413) and adding the flag to not set the dirty bit (the `None`) option, on our `SyncableMPTTTreeManager` or whatever we're calling it.

issue_title: PicklingError: Can't pickle <class 'html5lib.html5parser.getPhases.<locals>.TextPhase'>: attribute lookup TextPhase on html5lib.html5parser failed
issue_body:
Pickling an `HTMLParser` object  fails.

The following test demonstrates the behavior I was expecting:

```
def test_pickle_parser():
    parser = HTMLParser()
    encoded = pickle.dumps(parser)
    decoded = pickle.loads(encoded)
    ...
```

This is failing because the `*Phase` classes are not defined at the top-level, but instead are defined inside the factory function `getPhases()`. (This pattern exists in the `treebuilders` directory as well.)

Unless there is a reason to never reuse a parser, I think the object should be pickleable.

issue_title: etree serializer fails when serializing lxml.html element containing a comment
issue_body:
Calling `html5lib.serialize()` fails when passing `tree='etree'` and an element containing comments created by `lxml.html`.

This _should_ work because `lxml.html`'s documentation [says](http://lxml.de/lxmlhtml.html) about itself:

> The main API is based on the lxml.etree API, and thus, on the ElementTree API.

Passing `tree='lxml'` doesn't work as a work-around in general because of this issue: https://github.com/html5lib/html5lib-python/issues/338

To reproduce:

    html = """<!DOCTYPE html>
    <html>
    <body>
    <!-- foo -->
    </body>
    </html>
    """
    element = lxml.html.document_fromstring(html)
    # Works.
    print(html5lib.serialize(element, tree='lxml'))
    # Fails with the exception below.
    print(html5lib.serialize(element, tree='etree'))

Here is the exception:

    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "test-html5lib.py", line 46, in <module>
        print(html5lib.serialize(element, tree='etree'))
      File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/serializer.py", line 78, in serialize
        return s.render(walker(input), encoding)
      File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/serializer.py", line 323, in render
        return "".join(list(self.serialize(treewalker)))
      File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/serializer.py", line 209, in serialize
        for token in treewalker:
      File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/filters/optionaltags.py", line 18, in __iter__
        for previous, token, next in self.slider():
      File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/filters/optionaltags.py", line 9, in slider
        for token in self.source:
      File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/treewalkers/base.py", line 94, in __iter__
        details = self.getNodeDetails(currentNode)
      File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/treewalkers/etree.py", line 61, in getNodeDetails
        assert isinstance(node.tag, string_types), type(node.tag)
    AssertionError: <class 'cython_function_or_method'>

I'm guessing this issue is caused by the type-checking being done in [this line](https://github.com/html5lib/html5lib-python/blob/984f934d30ba2fde0e9ce5d4581c73fb81654474/html5lib/treewalkers/etree.py#L57):

    elif node.tag == ElementTreeCommentType:
        return base.COMMENT, node.text

Instead of exact type-checking which is more fragile, I wonder if some form of duck-typing can be used or using something in the ElementTree API itself.

issue_title: Probably invalid version checking in container creation
issue_body:
Hi.
I've stumbled upon something i think is a not up-to-date code.

utils.py at line 794 in my distribution (1.6.0) states following:

    if compare_version('1.10', version) >= 0:
        message = ('{0!r} parameter has no effect on create_container().'
                   ' It has been moved to start()')
        if dns is not None:
            raise errors.InvalidVersion(message.format('dns'))
        if volumes_from is not None:
            raise errors.InvalidVersion(message.format('volumes_from'))

As far as i can read from docs, this is not true - both Dns and VolumesFrom are seen in HostConfig for API 1.21. Even if everything is okay with this code, there is still a problem i'm struggling to solve - some of the HostConfig options can be specified in `.container_create()`, but not on `.start()`, e.g. logging driver and options may be set only during creation, but if i need to use custom DNS, i have to specify it on `.start()` call, and this will override whole HostConfig, erasing my logging configuration. Since this code directly raise exceptions, i have no option but to create whole dict directly and pass it to `container_from_configuration()`.

I'm sorry i'm not doing direct pull request instead of issue, but i don't have enough time to figure out correct version constraints and get into projects conventions - sadly, i'm no pythonist and can't do it.

issue_title: NpipeSocket.settimeout - Blocking and non-blocking are mixed up
issue_body: `settimeout(None)` puts the socket in non-blocking mode, `settimeout(0)` in blocking mode. Specification indicates it should be the other way around.

issue_title: Remove NI-SCOPE properties that are only useful with Complex Waveforms
issue_body:
Becase nimi-python doesn't support complex waveforms (see #189), we should not include properties that are only useful in conjunction of fetching Complex Waveforms.

* ddc_center_frequency
* ddc_data_processing_mode
* ddc_enabled
* ddc_frequency_translation_enabled
* ddc_frequency_translation_phase_i
* ddc_frequency_translation_phase_q
* ddc_q_source
* digital_gain
* digital_offset
* dither_enabled
* fetch_interleaved_iq_data
* fractional_resample_enabled <--- confirm not useful except with complex waveforms
* overflow_error_reporting <--- has enum, verify it goes away

issue_title: Characterize waveform fetch performance.
issue_body: We are concerned that copying samples from a list will be a performance bottleneck. We need to characterize this and decide if we need to make changes.

issue_title: Remove warning from readme prior to release
includes non-free autobahn.min.js without source
issue_body:
Hi. I'm the person packaging autobahn-python in Debian (for its use by OpenStack).

The current version of autobahn-python includes autobahn.min.js without even providing its non-minified version. From a distribution perspective, this is a non-free blob and missing source code, just like for a C object file. Please replace the non-free autobahn.min.js by its non-minified free software counter-part autobahn.js (and eventually offer a way to compress it at build time).

issue_title: Deprecate Python 2.6 support
issue_body:
Since one of our dependencies (Twisted) has dropped Python 2.6 support, and another (Cryptography.io) has deprecated it (https://github.com/pyca/cryptography/blob/master/src/cryptography/__init__.py#L21), and it is getting super super long in the tooth, maybe we should consider phasing out support next release.

Opinions, @oberstet, @meejah ?

issue_title: Integration with python/pytest logging functionality
issue_body:
#### I'm submitting a ... 
  - [x] feature request

#### What is the current behavior?
logger calls from python logging module are not shown on the report.

#### What is the expected behavior?
they be shown on the report

#### What is the motivation / use case for changing the behavior?
the report should contain as much info as possible. Log calls are very relevant information.

#### Please tell us about your environment:

- Allure version:        2.6.0
- Test framework:        pytest@3.4
- Allure adaptor:        allure-pytest@2.3.2b1

#### Other information 

I actually have a simple working workaround for this, by defining a custom logging handler for Allure:

```
import logging
import allure

class AllureLoggingHandler(logging.Handler):
    def log(self, message):
        with allure.step('Log {}'.format(message)):
	        pass

    def emit(self, record):
        self.log("({}) {}".format(record.levelname, record.getMessage()))

logger = logging.getLogger()
logger.addHandler(AllureLoggingHandler())

def test_print():
    logging.info("Logging an info message")
    logging.debug("Logging a DEBUG message")
    logging.warning("Sample time is too low!")
    raise Exception
```
It can be added for the test like this example or easily added also in the config section of a custom pytest plugin. Not sure if this could be merged into allure pytest plugin though.

It is still missing more complete formatting (module name). Also the handler log level is not dynamically adjusted as done in the pytest logging plugin, but it seems to work fine like this because pytest sets both the handler and the logger to the same level.

It even works with pytest logging switches. For instance, if we run this test with --log_level=INFO, the debug log record won't appear:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20566367/36359007-e2fdffc8-1516-11e8-9c81-d06b704c18f3.png)

issue_title: Questions on increase-nf_conntrack-connections.service
issue_body:
There's service `increase-nf_conntrack-connections.service` installed by Deis. On new CoreOS installation it fails because there's no conntrack module loaded by default in CoreOS 557. I have some questions/proposals regarding it. Here's what (as it seems to me) should be changed, please let me know if I misunderstand the purpose of this service.

1. Conntrack connections limit should persist after reboot. This can be done by either installing `increase-nf_conntrack-connections.service` or by adding following to `write_files:` section:

```
# /etc/sysctld.d/conntrack.conf
net.netfilter.nf_conntrack_max=262144
```

2. Same for module `nf_conntrack`. It should be either loaded in the service before calling `sysctl` or added as a file:

```
# /etc/modules-load.d/conntrack.conf
nf_conntrack
```

issue_title: Context properties of custom Channel ignored in LoggingHandler
issue_body:
I'm trying to set up a LoggingHandler with a few context properties but this is impossible currently:
```py
import logging
from applicationinsights import channel
from applicationinsights.logging import LoggingHandler

channel = channel.TelemetryChannel()
channel.context.properties['foo'] = 'bar'
handler = LoggingHandler(key, telemetry_channel=channel)
logging.basicConfig(handlers=[handler], format='%(message)s', level=logging.DEBUG)

logging.info("foo") # trace does not include foo property
logging.shutdown()
```

The reason this is not working is that LoggingHandler constructs a new TelemetryClient instance which then ignores the existing context from the passed channel object:
```py
class TelemetryClient(object):
    def __init__(self, instrumentation_key, telemetry_channel=None):
        ...
        self._context = channel.TelemetryContext()
        self._context.instrumentation_key = instrumentation_key
        self._channel = telemetry_channel or channel.TelemetryChannel()

    @property
    def context(self):
        return self._context
```

Shouldn't this be instead:
```py
class TelemetryClient(object):
    def __init__(self, instrumentation_key, telemetry_channel=None):
        self._channel = telemetry_channel or channel.TelemetryChannel()
        self.context.instrumentation_key = instrumentation_key

    @property
    def context(self):
        return self._channel.context
```
?

issue_title: Add a new `Configurable` base class
Add logging output for chain reorgs
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Chain re-orgs happen and it would be nice to see some info about them when running trinity.

### How can it be fixed

Whenever trinity is syncing and it encounters a chain reorg we should log something with depth and hashes.  Probably configurable to a certain minimum depth.

For lightchain, this should be relatively easy.

https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/master/p2p/lightchain.py#L214

Anywhere that `headerdb.coro_persist_header` is called should return an iterable of headers which are *new* to the canonical chain.  If the returned list contains more than 1 entry then the chain just re-organized.

Doing this for full sync is going to require updates to the Py-EVM API so that `chain.import_block` returns an iterable of blocks that are part of the new canonical chain.

issue_title: Add `getNextLog` and `fetch_candidate_head`
issue_body:
### What is wrong?
Add `getNextLog` and `fetch_candidate_head` described in https://github.com/ethereum/sharding/blob/develop/docs/doc.md#fetch-candidate-heads-in-reverse-sorted-order

### How can it be fixed
Implement `getNextLog` with web3.py(essentially `LogFilter.get_new_entries` acts like that) and `fetch_candidate_head` in `MainchainHandler`.

issue_title: Changes to `Chain.import_block`
issue_body:
Blocks: https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/issues/733

### What is wrong?

Currently `chain.import_block(..)` returns the block that was imported.  Sometimes, this will cause a chain re-org, but this API doesn't expose sufficient information to determine this.

### How can it be fixed

Change `Chain.import_block` to return an iterable of blocks which are *new* to the canonical chain.  Then callers can inspect the return value and determine that a re-org happened when more than a single block is returned.

issue_title: Wagon fails to validate correct wagons
issue_body:
This is actually probably 2 issues. 

### Issue 1
Steps to reproduce:
* wagon create pip
* wagon validate <RESULTING_WAGON>
The validation will fail.

### Issue 2
The steps to reproduce are the same, but here the cause is clear - after pip install `_`s change to `-`s, and so [this](https://github.com/cloudify-cosmo/wagon/blob/master/wagon.py#L461) fails.
For example, [here's](https://github.com/cloudify-cosmo/cloudify-manager/tree/master/tests/integration_tests/resources/plugins/target_aware_mock) a package on which create/validate will fail.

issue_title: Conda install --clone doesn't respect dependency order
issue_body: For example, there are gcc-4.8 and gcc-5 packages that test whether gcc works on `post-link` signal, but since not all gcc dependencies get cloned before this signal is fired, the tests fail.

issue_title: Incorrect result for np.rint on Win32, numpy 1.10.1, MKL
issue_body:
Sorry for the repetition - I just reported this to numpy : https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/6685

However, it seems to be an issue specific to MKL, and therefore to the (only?) conda numpy packages for Windows 32-bit.

With 32-bit numpy + MKL on Windows:

```
>>> import numpy as np
>>> ds = 4607998452777363968
>>> int(float(ds))
4607998452777363968L

I do not get this error from:
- conda 32-bit + numpy 1.9.3;
- conda 64-bit + numpy 1.10.1;
- Python.org Python + the sourceforge numpy 1.10.1 superpack installers;

I do get the same error from the (now removed?) Christoph Gohlke numpy 1.10.1 32-bit MKL wheels.

issue_title: Race condition for root environment detection
issue_body:
Periodically, when two conda processes are running at the same time, it is possible to see a race condition on determining whether the root environment is writable. Notice how the following produces two different configs from the same setup:

```
$ conda info & conda info 

Current conda install:

             platform : osx-64
        conda version : 3.18.3

                        /Users/pelson/envs
                        /Users/pelson/miniconda/envs
        package cache : /Users/pelson/.conda/envs/.pkgs

                        /Users/pelson/miniconda/pkgs
...

Current conda install:

             platform : osx-64
        conda version : 3.18.3
  conda-build version : 1.18.0
       python version : 2.7.10.final.0
     requests version : 2.8.1
     root environment : /Users/pelson/miniconda  (writable)
  default environment : /Users/pelson/miniconda
     envs directories : /Users/pelson/miniconda/envs
        package cache : /Users/pelson/miniconda/pkgs
...
```

The offending line is in https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/master/conda/config.py#L135-L143 and https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/master/conda/utils.py#L43-L54. My assumption is that the ```.conda-try-write``` is being removed by the other process, and the exception is being raised in the ```finally``` block.

issue_title: Help with RPATH on OS X with cmake
issue_body:
See the OS X (Travis CI) failure here: https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/pull/1732

For some reason, the `@loader_path` rpath set when the libraries are being linked is not being respected after the conda build -- the C++ shared library in question, `libpyarrow.dylib`, is in the same directory as the Cython modules. CMake is linking the transitive dependencies of `libpyarrow` so that might be a problem -- I can experiment if specifying `LINK_PRIVATE` in the `target_link_libraries` helps. If any CMake experts know the right incantation that would be most helpful.

issue_title: conda=4.1 no longer respects HTTP_PROXY
issue_body:
My proxy variables are properly set:
```
$ set https_proxy
HTTPS_PROXY=http://myproxy:8080
```

``conda=4.0.8`` successfully picks up my ``%HTTPS_PROXY%`` and installs ``conda=4.1``:

```
$ conda install conda=4.1
Fetching package metadata: ..........
Solving package specifications: .........

The following packages will be UPDATED:
    conda:     4.0.8-py27_0 defaults --> 4.1.0-py27_0 defaults
```

now trying to revert back to ``conda=4.0``:
```
$ conda install conda=4.0
Fetching package metadata .........
Could not connect to https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/win-64/
Connection error: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='repo.continuum.io', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url...
```
proxy settings no longer work. Configuring ``proxy_servers`` in ``.condarc`` restores connectivity.
