issue_title: Check compiler seems to only check for return code, not for result
issue_body:
### Description

I *think* this was different before. I get the "compiler works" message although it doesn't.

snip from the log:

~~~~
09:21:55:937::DEBUG::5220::open_cobol_ide.compilers::output: 'Das System kann den angegebenen Pfad nicht finden.\r\n'
09:21:55:937::DEBUG::5220::open_cobol_ide.compilers::exit code: 0
09:21:55:938::INFO::5220::open_cobol_ide.compilers::GnuCOBOL compiler check: success
09:21:55:938::INFO::5220::open_cobol_ide.compilers::Executable extension: .exe
09:21:55:939::INFO::5220::open_cobol_ide.compilers::Module extension: .dll
~~~~

The first line means "the specified path cannot be found". The reason that the exit code is still zero is because of a wrapper shell that calls the actual cobc command and does not return the error.
The reason that I believe that is a bug is: OCIDE should check if there is an actual compiled result.

### System information

- Operating System: Windows
- OpenCobolIDE: 4.7.3.dev24
- GnuCOBOL: 
- Python: 3.4.3 (32bits)
- Qt: 5.5.1
- PyQt: 5.5.1
- pyqode.core: 2.9.dev

issue_title: Add index type filter to show and delete indices command
issue_body:
I have a usecase where I need to delete a specific type of some indices instead of all the data.

This can be done by using the [delete_mapping](https://elasticsearch-py.readthedocs.org/en/master/api.html#elasticsearch.client.IndicesClient.delete_mapping) method.

IIUC, `curator.api.delete_indices` and `curator.api.delete` need to be extended with a `types` argument and `curator.cli.index_selection.indices` needs to have a new `@click.option` decorator.

If this is an OK way to implement, I can create a PR.

issue_title: `az network application-gateway frontend-port create` takes a long time to finish
issue_body:
We're trying to create an application gateway that has multiple ports open (one for HTTP and one for HTTPS)

Currently we have set up the gateway using the old azure client, with args like this
```
azure network application-gateway create [...] --http-listener-protocol https --frontend-port 443
```

When we try to automate creating an additional listener on this gateway, creating a `frontend-port` seems to take forever:

```
$ az network application-gateway frontend-port create [...] -name frontendPort02 --port 80 --verbose
Starting long running operation 'Starting network application-gateway frontend-port create'
```

If we do an `az network application-gateway frontend-port list`, we see the port has been created but the `provisioningState` is `Updating`, even after something like 10-20 minutes.  It takes more than 20 minutes for the state to move to finished.  

Are we doing this wrong?  Is there another way we could be doing this?  Is there a way we could create multiple front-end listeners when we create an application gateway?

Is it actually not finished until after 20 minutes, or is the API being extra conservative about reporting something in 'Updating' vs 'Succeeded' state?

issue_title: Specifying 'disabled' for '--endpoint-status' creates an endpoint in the 'Enabled' state
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
The '--endpoint-status' argument value of 'az network traffic-manager endpoint create' is case sensitive and does not behave the way one would expect. If 'Disabled' is specified as the value, the new endpoint has 'Disabled' as the status. If 'disabled' is specified, the new endpoint has 'Enabled' as the status.

**To Reproduce**
Create a new traffic manager endpoint using 'az network traffic-manager endpoint create' and specify '--endpoint-status disabled' as one of the arguments. Observe that the newly created endpoint is in the 'Enabled' state even though 'disabled' was requested.

**Expected behavior**
Specifying an invalid value for the endpoint status such as 'invalid' will cause the Azure CLI to print the message that the only valid values are 'Enabled' and 'Disabled'. But the CLI will accept 'disabled' as a value but then treat it as if it was actually 'Enabled'. Either the CLI should reject 'disabled' as invalid or it should accept it and treat it as 'Disabled'.

**Environment summary**
Azure CLI 2.0.33 on both Windows and Linux

issue_title: az vm extension set requires a publisher, but there is no way (command) to get the publishers
issue_body:
In order to set the vm extension, you need to specify a publisher
`usage: az vm extension set [-h] [--output {json,tsv,list,table,jsonc}]
                           [--verbose] [--debug] [--query JMESPATH]
                           [--vm-name NAME]
                           [--resource-group RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME] --publisher
                           PUBLISHER [--settings SETTINGS]
                           [--ids RESOURCE_ID [RESOURCE_ID ...]] [--name NAME]
                           [--no-auto-upgrade] [--version VERSION]
                           [--protected-settings PROTECTED_SETTINGS]
az vm extension set: error: argument --publisher is required`

But there is no way to determine the available publishers or the extension images they publish.

issue_title: VM encryption across resource groups does not work
issue_body:
### Environment summary

Install Method homebrew / 2.0.19 / macOS 10.12.6 / zsh

I am attempting to add encryption to existing VMs. I have had no troubles when the VM & the key vault are in the same resource group. However, when I try to use the key vault to encrypt VMs outside of its resource group, the request fails. 

Call:
```
az vm encryption enable \
  --resource-group my-rg \
  --name my-vm \
  --aad-client-id my-sp-id \
  --aad-client-secret my-sp-secret \
  --disk-encryption-keyvault /subscriptions/my-sub/resourceGroups/my-other-rg/providers/Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/my-vault \
  --key-encryption-key my-key \
  --volume-type all
```

Result:
```
Key Vault https://my-vault.vault.azure.net/keys/my-key either has not been enabled for Volume Encryption or the vault id provided does not match /subscriptions/my-sub/resourceGroups/my-rg/providers/Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/my-other-rgs-vault-name's true resource id.
```

This does not make sense to me. I put the full resource ID to the key vault in my request but the error is talking about the URL to my key vault not matching a non-existant key vault in the VM's resource group.

When the switch-over occurs, the IP address of my device stays unchanged (as that is on the local network and assigned through DHCP).

Not sure if it is out of scope of the project, but it would be handy to have some kind of `--print-diff` option that simply prints a diff of the files on failure. It would generally only be useful in CI when you can't just go back and inspect what changed.

```
Reordering imports in pre_commit/main.py

issue_title: pip install pybullet works,  but import pybullet fails
issue_body:
I have sucessfully  installed pybullet with pip (on Windows 10, python 3.6, Anaconda3). The following commands also works sucessfully: import pybullet_envs and pybullet_utils. However when I try import pybullet or import pybullet_data, the following error appears: " ImportError: DLL load failed: The specified module could not be found."

There is a way to fix this issue? Thank you!

issue_title: Sharing db connection to nested nested app
issue_body:
## Long story short

I've read  your answer in #2413 (raised in #2412 ).
The solution (`subapp['db'] = mainapp['db']`) does not work when it comes to nested subapps.
For example, if I have to build an app A, which have some subapp B and I need to pass the db connection from A to B, which can be done in that behavior. But, when I need to nest app A as a subapp to C, another app, and how should I pass db from C to A and to B. Clearly, the sharing between A and B exists before that between C and A, when A does not know the existence of db (which is even set inside startup) and C should also know nothing about the inner structure of A. A more complicated mechanism should be provided. 

## Expected behaviour

A more complicated solution. 

## Actual behaviour

Too simple to solve the problem. 

## Steps to reproduce

```python
from aiohttp import web

app_b = web.Application()

app_a = web.Application()
app_b['db'] = app_a['db']  # error
app_a.add_subapp('/b', app_b)

app_c = web.Application()
app_c['db'] = 'db'  # actually inside a startup event
app_c.add_subapp('/a', app_a)  # how should I pass app_c['db']

web.run_app(app_c)

```
## My solution
I'm trying to solve the problem by making the db become a `promise` (or a monad, which makes more sense about the side effect of the main app on the sub app ?). 
```python
from aiohttp import web


class MyApplication(web.Application):

    get_db = None

    def set_db(self, db):
        self.get_db = lambda: db

    def add_subapp(self, prefix, subapp):
        # The db should be a `promise` (like a monad).
        # When the `db` property is called,
        # the promise is then calculated.
        # So, when the db is set, a promise is set,
        # and when it is passed to the subapp, the promise is passed

        async def set_db(app):
            app.get_db = lambda: self.get_db()
        subapp.on_startup.append(set_db)
        #  subapp.get_db = lambda: self.get_db()
        return super().add_subapp(prefix, subapp)

```

If we use statements DROP/CREATE with escaping, i.e.
```
async def query(query, args):

            await cur.execute(query, args)

```
Am I doing something wrong?

issue_title: [Feature request] Each cell keeps track of execution time
issue_body:
Where ever possible, cells can keep track of execution time (say to the nearest microsecond). I don't want anything complex like `%time` or `%timeit, but it would be neat if each cell also stored when execution began, and when ended. Basically, in a `.ipnb` file, add key : value to store start and end times, and display it when mouse hovers over the cell execution count. Something similar to this:

```
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "metadata": {
    "collapsed": false,
    "scrolled": true,
    "start_exec": <start datetime>
    "end_exec": <end datetime>
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "print("foo")",
    "print("bar")"
   ]
  },
```

When displaying, it could subtract end time with start time, and also show the execution time for the cell. For advanced and accurate run timing, `%time` will be available. Therefore, this will be a crude or approximate time keeping.

This is useful to know how long a cell run roughly took to execute. I process billions of records on a daily basis, developing the code using Jupyter, and then moving it to a Python script for headless execution. Usually while developing in Jupyter, I work with a very small subset of the data, and often times try to keep a track of run times, so that I get an idea of how long the whole dataset will take. Even when I do, I am only interested in the nearest minute or second, because when my cells run for over 10 minutes, microsecond or nanosecond accuracy is immaterial.

My current approach is to note down the time when I press `Ctrl+Enter` and then stare at the screen till the run counter changes from `*` to a number, and note the time again. For a long run, for which I don't know when it might end, it is not a feasible method. Therefore, if the start time was set to when execute instruction is detected, and end time is set to when the counter number is incremented, and these times displayed when mouse hovers over the run counter (along with the difference between the two), it would be very helpful. Thanks.

issue_title: Remove `ModelType`
issue_body:
This thing seems useless to me. I wanna remove it.

I think it'd be more interesting to have `Model` instances behave like `BaseTypes` and thus not require special behavior or wrapping.

I am thinking out loud on this one and asking for feedback.

Specifically, is there any value to having `ModelType`?

With:

```python
class Bar(Model):
    b = StringType()

class Foo(Model):
    f = ModelType(Bar)
```

Or, and this is the one I prefer:

```python
class Bar(Model):
    b = StringType()

class Foo(Model):
    f = Bar()
```

If implemented correctly, a call to `f.to_native()` would produce the same output in both cases.

issue_title: Advice speeding up autograd for loss functions with expensive inner loop?
issue_body:
I have autograd working on a loss function for a pretty large scale problem... essentially a version of topic models for text documents. All seems to be working fine, except the performance seems to be a bit too slow for my application: on toy data, if I compute the loss function in X seconds for 100 documents, evaluating the gradient can take about 25-100X seconds. Rumor has it that usually, 2-3X might be the expected cost, so I'm wondering:

* (1) is 25X slowness unusual? or is it ok?
* (2) any advice on writing code differently to speed things up?

Below is a minimal "non-working" example to give the flavor of the problem. The overall loss function works out to a sum over documents, where at each document we perform many iterations (typically 25-100) of an inner loop that iteratively estimates a needed vector (more iterates = higher quality) . Every line of code is using vectorized operations as much as possible. The truly expensive part based on my profiling is just that the inner while loop at each document requires many iterations.

```
def calc_loss_for_many_docs(topics_KV):
    loss = 0.0
    for d in xrange(n_docs):
        vector_d_K = calc_vector_for_single_doc(topics_KV[:, specific_words_d_U])
        loss = loss - inner(weights_K, vector_d_K)
    return loss

def calc_vector_for_single_doc(topics_KU):
    ct_topics_KU = topics_KU * word_ct_d_U[np.newaxis, :]
    K = topics_KU.shape[0]
    pi_d_K = np.ones(K) / float(K)
    # Start loop over iterations
    giter = 0
    while giter < max_iters:
        giter = giter + 1
        denom_U = 1.0 / np.dot(pi_d_K, topics_KU)
        grad_K = pi_step_size * np.dot(ct_topics_KU, denom_U)
        pi_d_K = pi_d_K * np.exp(grad_K)
        pi_d_K_sum = np.sum(pi_d_K)
        pi_d_K = pi_d_K / pi_d_K_sum
    return pi_d_K
```

I'm happy to give more details and real working code examples if that would be helpful, but I thought at first I'd just generally ask for quick ideas about this. My other experience with Python leads me to believe that the inner while loop is always a bad sign and that moving to Cython/C++ is needed. But I don't want to lose autograd magic that way.

Thanks!

Packages must have at least one pure python module
issue_body:
Due to how the build extension modules are shipped as "package data", they are not shipped because:

  https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/29c8385814335e91538d76b68578b6c0ab483c1a/setuptools/command/sdist.py#L147

One approach is to find some way to override distutils.dist.Distribution.has_pure_modules().

Another approach would check that there is at least one pure python module, and warn the user that this bug exists.

Another approach is to stop marking the generated C-extension as "package data", list them as the output of a custom *build_ext* command, and make sure they get added to the package manifest.

issue_title: encoding of \u2028 and \u2029 is incorrect
issue_body:
the [spec](https://matrix.org/docs/spec/appendices.html#canonical-json) says that `\u2028` (line separator) should be encoded as the UTF-8 `\xe2\x80\xa8`, however python-canonicaljson encodes it as the `\u`-escaped version.

This is largely thanks to https://github.com/simplejson/simplejson/issues/206.

Link fate of nodes in subclusters
issue_body:
Depends on #703

Related to #644

1) Assume there is a Toil cluster with one or more Spark/HDFS subclusters. If one of the Spark/HDFS master processes fails, the service job representing it should also fail (#703).

2) When that service job fails, the service jobs that represent the Spark/HDFS slaves connected to that Spark/HDFS master should fail. As it is now I think the slave processes and their representative services will just keep running (this issue).

3) When the master service job fails, the driver job using it will fail.

4) On a subcluster with replication factor R, if the right combination of R Spark/HDFS workers die, a block of HDFS data is lost. This leads to driver failure but it may take a while before that situation is discovered.

5) If the driver job fails, the entire cluster should be taken down (#644).

issue_title: Set default version
issue_body:
The `ServerConfig` class does not currently require that the "version" attribute be provided. This is problematic when an entity must make an assumption about what version the server is running. For example, entities have different sets of fields based on what version the server is running, so of course an assumption must be made - but what version should be assumed?

Update method `ServerConfig.__init__`. If no version is passed in when instantiating a `ServerConfig` object, then some version should be assumed. I would support assuming version '1!0' (epoch 1, version 0). This version will be greater than any version provided by Satellite, thus letting us assume that a user is running a development version of Satellite by default. This will work so long as Satellite retains its current versioning scheme.

issue_title: Automatically mark async test functions
issue_body:
I have a test suite that uses pytest-asyncio for some of its tests and it works pretty well. Thank you for creating such a useful plugin.

Enhancement Request
------------------------

All of my coroutine test functions are declared with `async`. To avoid marking every such function with `@pytest.mark.asyncio`, in the root `conftest.py` for that suite I have added the following pytest hook:

```python
import pytest
import inspect

def pytest_collection_modifyitems(session, config, items):
    for item in items:
        if isinstance(item, pytest.Function) and inspect.iscoroutinefunction(item.function):
            item.add_marker(pytest.mark.asyncio)
```

So far I have not managed to find any drawback to doing this sort of thing - the `async` keyword indicates coroutine functions just as clearly as the decorator does, and this approach seems to correctly mark each async test. 

Could something like this be a part of pytest-asyncio itself, or is there a rationale for not including such a feature? 

I understand that this project existed before Python had `async`/`await` semantics; however, you appear to have dropped support for Python < 3.5 (#57), which means that every supported Python version also supports the `async` keyword. Furthermore, I am not suggesting the removal or deprecation of the decorator approach since there are probably valid use cases for creating coroutines without using `async`.

If this kind of feature would be appreciated, I can contribute the changes myself when I can find the time.

I'm trying to write some addition tests for `oscar.apps.partner.strategy.FixedRateTax`, but I've hit a stumbling block:

issue_title: Basket.line_tax returns None
issue_body:
Due to the change in ad1094c1e8, if a line's tax is set to `Decimal('0.00')`, `Line.is_tax_known` will return `True`, but `Line.line_tax` will return `None`. Formerly in this case, `Line.line_tax` returned `Decimal('0.00')` instead of `None`. I'd suggest making `line_tax` check `is_tax_known` instead of doing it's own check.

```
 @property
     def line_tax(self):
-         if self.unit_tax:
+         if self.is_tax_known:
             return self.quantity * self.unit_tax
``` 

See [ad1094c1e8:src/oscar/apps/basket/abstract_models.py:828](https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/commit/ad1094c1e89c0314c0a883b0b0a4e618a443da22#diff-519bc8e8997795240c2a90d8d63b66baL828)

issue_title: Improved image handling - tracking issue
issue_body:
There are a lot of tickets at the moment related to image handling, most of which are interrelated. Here's a list of the various issues that I can think of:

- [ ]  Allow per-variant product images (#1611, #1643).
- [ ]  Fix broken image ordering in dashboard (#2289) and improve the image upload generally to be more user friendly.
- [ ]  Allow reordering of images from dashboard (#2287).
- [ ] Figure out a better way to handle the "missing image" image (#2168).
- [ ]  Make it possible for projects to use a different thumbnailing solution (#1408).
- [ ] Allow image attributes (?).
- [ ] Build in support for retina images

There are a few PRs currently open to address some of these, but I think we need to be mindful of the overall aims (e.g., the JS proposed in #2287 is specific to the current dashboard, which is going to change). The dashboard refactor in #2302 will be important to several of these issues.

Broadly I think there are three main tasks:

1. Refactor models to allow better variant support.
2. Refactor dashboard UI for handling images.
3. Move thumbnailing logic into a standalone module that can be overridden. At the same time also add support for retina thumbnails etc.

issue_title: Suggestion: Dotnet core project template to support Grpc with DI and additional middle-wares
issue_body:
# Issue Title
dotnet core has some basic template to work with MVC based web api's. These template supports many things like DI and support for various middle-wares. But no template as such to support Grpc along with DI and middle-wares. 

# General

My Development Environment:
dotnet core 2.1.301
ubuntu 16.04

What I am trying to do is to leverage the web api template to use the Grpc, in order to make use of the features like DI, middle-wares etc. But the way I am doing is bit ugly, I myself is not satisfied with it. I did refer to some of the git repositories which has some solution like:

https://github.com/m-andrei/grpc-example-for-.net-core/tree/master/Grpc.Example.Server

but still, I think it will be a good idea to have some standard/official dotnet core project template with combined support for following:
1. Grpc
2. DI
3. Middle-wares (tracing, monitoring etc)

issue_title: BrotliStream crashes trying to decompress ~2000000 int32 array
UWP app store submission fails due to certification after 3rd party library integration
issue_body:
Hi,

- I have got an app which is already on the store (for QA) and I am trying to submit an update for after integrating a 3rd party voip library. 
- Submission failed due to certification problem caused by the 3rd party library apparently (please see attached screenshot)

![store rejecting voip lib](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5698730/15189337/03846a9e-17a2-11e6-80d8-bc38d1c66d4f.JPG)


- The store suggests the certification tool be used to fix any signature issue. However, the certification tool does not work as it only takes Xap, Appx or AppxBundle while the binary I’m trying to upload is AppxUpload (which was working fine before)
- Link recommended to follow (from the store submission logs): https://msdn.microsoft.com/library/windows/apps/hh694081.aspx
- I suspect the issue happened when I decided to set AppxPackageSigningEnabled to false in MSBuild command (it was true before and submission used to work fine). If that flag was kept to true (after integrating the voip library), the MSBuild command would fail (please see attached screenshot).

- The command used was: 
MSBuild .\MyApp.sln /t:Rebuild /p:Configuration=Release /p:Platform=ARM /p:PreBuildEvent= /p:AppxPackageIsForStore=true /p:UapAppxPackageBuildMode=CI /p:AppxPackageSigningEnabled=false

![msbuild signature failure](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5698730/15189342/0dc1bc1e-17a2-11e6-8479-9d6720aaafb9.JPG)


- The bit that is failing in the voip library is a service (that runs in the background). I was using the same main project certificate to sign it before I decided to turn off the AppxPackageSigningEnabled flag since the approach did not work!
- I am not sure whether there is any requirement third-party libraries need to fulfill. Is there anything we, as developers, need to know (and forward to that voip library tech team) regarding a third-party service certification for store submission? Or is it something I am doing wrong in the MSBuild command?


Please feel free to move this issue to any category you think is more suitable (can't find store-related issue entry).

Any help would be appreciated :)

Thanks!
Hisham

issue_title: --keep-until-expiring requires interaction if no certificate updateupdate
issue_body:
Running a command of `./letsencrypt-auto --config /opt/letsencrypt.ini certonly --keep-until-expiring --agree-tos -d my.domain.com ` with configuration file contents that includes the option `text = True` results in prompted input if certificates are not yet due for removal, breaking automated scripts.

## STR:
1. Request certificates using let's encrypt (using the options provided below).
2. Request certificates again using `--keep-until-expiring` flag set and a configuration file that specifies `text = True`, the same that should have been used for the first request:
  2. An example command would be `./letsencrypt-auto --config /opt/letsencrypt.ini certonly --keep-until-expiring --agree-tos -d my.domain.com`
  2. with a configuration file of:
  ```
# Let's Encrypt Configuration with defaults
# All flags used by the client can be configured here. Run Let's Encrypt with
# "--help" to learn more about the available options.

# Use a 4096 bit RSA key instead of 2048
rsa-key-size = 4096

# Uncomment and update to register with the specified e-mail address
email = EMAIL_HERE@EMAIL_HERE

# Uncomment and update to generate certificates for the specified
# domains.
# domains = example.com, www.example.com
# Always provide domains via env. See letsencrypt-run.sh

# Uncomment to use a text interface instead of ncurses
text = True

# Uncomment to use the standalone authenticator on port 443
# authenticator = standalone
# standalone-supported-challenges = tls-sni-01

# Uncomment to use the webroot authenticator. Replace webroot-path with the
# path to the public_html / webroot folder being served by your web server.
authenticator = webroot
webroot-path = /var/www/challenges
```


## Expected:
1. Let's Encrypt client prints:
```
------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
Certificate not yet due for renewal; no action taken.                           
------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
```
and does not prompt for input afterward, allowing the client to exit with a code of `0`.

## Actual:
1. Let's Encrypt client prints:
```
------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
Certificate not yet due for renewal; no action taken.                           
------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
Press Enter to Continue
```
and requires interaction from a tty before exiting cleanly. 


## Environment
- OS = Ubuntu 14.04.2 
- Webserver = nginx, but using certonly since acme-challenges are successfully handled through an existing nginx server. The `letsencrypt.ini` file specifies the correct challenge directory for the client by providing the options 
  - 
  ```
authenticator = webroot
webroot-path = /var/www/challenges
```

### Notes
Let's Encrypt was installed by running and then calling:
```
git clone https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt
./letsencrypt-auto --config /opt/letsencrypt.ini certonly --keep-until-expiring --agree-tos -d my.domain.com
```

issue_title: Update docs to show from_email formats
issue_body:
You can supply a friendly from in the existing `from_email` field. For example the following are both valid:

```python
sp.transmissions.send(..., from_email='testing@example.com', ...)
```

```python
sp.transmissions.send(..., from_email='Test Email <testing@example.com>', ...)
```

The `_extract_recipients` (see [this line](https://github.com/SparkPost/python-sparkpost/blob/master/sparkpost/transmissions.py#L99)) method handles converting either of these into the proper format for the API. We just need to document it.

issue_title: InvalidOperation ConversionSyntax after replacing float with Decimal (Refs #570)
issue_body:
Hey there,
Thanks for a great package!

We encountered an issue with 2.7.0 release and exactly this change (float to Decimal):
https://github.com/dateutil/dateutil/pull/570/files#diff-4b65e7edb2b73639d2d3360fa8d05343R937

Before release it was float and producing `ValueError`:
```
>>> float('TEST')
ValueError: could not convert string to float: 'TEST'
```
After release, same operation goes with `Decimal`, which produces a different error:
```
>>> Decimal('TEST')
decimal.InvalidOperation: [<class 'decimal.ConversionSyntax'>]
```

I am not 100% familiar with `dateutil` code, but I see, that `ValueError` was covered in `dateutil.parser._parser.parser#_parse` here: https://github.com/dateutil/dateutil/blob/master/dateutil/parser/_parser.py#L689-L825

But now that piece is raising `decimal.InvalidOperation: [<class 'decimal.ConversionSyntax'>]` which is not covered at all and breaking the code, that uses it.


What shall we do in that case? Shall we wrap it in `try/except` like it says in leftovers `TODO` comment?


My environment (just in case):
```
>>> import dateutil; print(dateutil.__version__)
2.7.0

13:16 $ python --version
Python 3.6.4
```

**Upd.** Code example with failing parser:

```
>dateutil.parser.parse('1: test')
decimal.InvalidOperation: [<class 'decimal.ConversionSyntax'>]

>dateutil.parser.parse('9999: 12, test')
decimal.InvalidOperation: [<class 'decimal.ConversionSyntax'>]
```

issue_title: Integrate custom.Types deeper in the library
issue_body:
> *This is a rather big change which probably needs more thought and is long-term.*

This would make it easier to use custom types everywhere accross the library. For instance, a `custom.Entity` that wrapped `User`, `Chat` and `Channel`. These entities can appear nearly everywhere, and replacing them by hand wherever a friendly method can return them is a pain.

Instead, `TLObject`'s could have a `def set_client(self, client)` or similar that they would override if they were aware of some special-cased objects. For instance, `messages.messageSlice` would know it has to set the client on its `messages`, `users` and and `chats`.

In the lower level, when the `BinaryReader` is about to read a known-special cased object, it would create an instance of that class. For instance, we could have:
```python
class Entity(TLObject):
    def online_within(self, ...):
        ...  # Convenience code

class User(Entity):  # Special to use the Entity
    ...
```

More than the reader, perhaps the code generator would need to know about this.

This would allow to get rid of the current hack for `custom.Message` to behave like either a `Message` or `MessageService`.

issue_title: What are folium natural dependencies ?
issue_body:
Just to open the question :

today, folium is *officially* depending on `Jinja2`, and the tests require `vincent`, `pandas` and eventually `mock` to run.

I wonder whether we shall extend the *official* dependency list ; provided that things like `numpy` are installed almost everywhere (?)

issue_title: In SpeechToTextV1.recognize(): Keyword argument 'word_confidence' is not propagated correctly to API
issue_body:
In the method `SpeechToTextV1.recognize()` the keyword argument `word_confidence` is not propagated correctly to Watson's API. If I run `examples/speech_to_text_v1.py`, a JSON object is printed to my terminal as expected. But if I add `word_confidence=True` to the argument list, I get a warning in the returned JSON object. Here is the code before adding `word_confidence`:

    …
    with open(join(dirname(__file__), '../resources/speech.wav'), 'rb') as audio_file:
        print(json.dumps(speech_to_text.recognize(
            audio_file, content_type='audio/wav', timestamps=True), indent=2))
    …

Here it is after adding `word_confidence`:

    …
    with open(join(dirname(__file__), '../resources/speech.wav'), 'rb') as audio_file:
        print(json.dumps(speech_to_text.recognize(
            audio_file, content_type='audio/wav', timestamps=True, word_confidence=True), indent=2))
    …

Running the modified python file yields

    …
    "warnings": [
        "Unknown arguments: wordConfidence."
    …

If in [speech_to_text_v1.py](https://github.com/watson-developer-cloud/python-sdk/blob/59f21de85bbdec88d839f369ec8e53afa94a087f/watson_developer_cloud/speech_to_text_v1.py#L44) I replace the line

    'wordConfidence': word_confidence,

by

    'word_confidence': word_confidence,

then the warning in the returned JSON object vanishes and the confidence score for each word is included in it.

issue_title: deserialize() throws exception even if strict=False
issue_body:
See https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/blob/2.0.0b4/marshmallow/marshalling.py#L254

Instead of adding the validation error to self.errors, `Unmarshaller` throws an exception directly at this line. This can happen, for example, if the data you're trying to deserialize is a list instead of a dictionary. I think this should be treated like any other validation error and not actually throw the exception here.

issue_title: Allow passing original value to validators of a specific field
issue_body:
## Description

I want to validate and deserialize an `DateTime` file according to [ISO8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format (`YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss+tz_hh:tz_mm`), so this is my code:

``` python
def validate_iso8601_datetime(value):
    pattern = (
        r'^(?P<year>[0-9]{4})-(?P<month>1[0-2]|0[1-9])-(?P<day>3[01]|0[1-9]|[12][0-9])'
        'T(?P<hour>2[0-3]|[01][0-9]):(?P<minute>[0-5][0-9]):(?P<second>[0-5][0-9])'
        '\+(?P<tz_hh>[0-9]{2}):(?P<tz_mm>[0-9]{2})$'
    )
    match = re.match(pattern, value)
    if not match:
        raise ValidationError('{} is not match the ISO8601 format.'.format(value))

class ReservationSchema(Schema):
    working_date = fields.DateTime(format="iso", validate=validate_iso8601_datetime)

data = {
    "working_date": "2015-06-07 22:00:00+07:00"
}
result, errors = ReservationSchema().load(data)

==> TypeError: expected string or bytes-like object
```

We have the `TypeError` exception because `value` passed to `validate_iso8601_datetime()` function is `datetime` type (not a `string` type).
So I think we should support a way to pass original data to validator function (`validate` param in field declaration)
## Proposed API

Allow passing original data to validator function:

``` python
Field(..., validate=validate_function, pass_original=False)
```

What do you guys think about this proposal?

issue_title: Issues pickling sympy.Function('f')
issue_body:
We have a test failure in our SymPy test suite from cloudpickle. I have bisected it to the cloudpickle commit https://github.com/cloudpipe/cloudpickle/commit/aec80d21ddff84cf2a83dce3cb5921a9f58ffd05

The failure is described at https://github.com/sympy/sympy/issues/15116

The basic failure is this:


```py
>>> import cloudpickle
>>> from sympy import Function
>>> f = Function('f')
>>> f1 = cloudpickle.loads(cloudpickle.dumps(f))
>>> f.__dict__['_prop_handler']
{'commutative': <function Function._eval_is_commutative at 0x1121f27b8>, 'negative': <function Expr._eval_is_negative at 0x112112d08>, 'positive': <function Expr._eval_is_positive at 0x112112c80>, 'complex': <function Function._eval_is_complex at 0x1121f2840>}
>>> f1.__dict__['_prop_handler']
{'commutative': <function Function._eval_is_commutative at 0x1130a7d08>, 'negative': <function Expr._eval_is_negative at 0x1130a7e18>, 'positive': <function Expr._eval_is_positive at 0x11309bbf8>, 'complex': <function Function._eval_is_complex at 0x1130aae18>}
>>> from sympy import Expr
>>> Expr._eval_is_negative
<function Expr._eval_is_negative at 0x112112d08>
```

The SymPy test tests that all the attributes are the same, and fails because cloudpickle creates new instances of the Expr methods. 

I'm not clear if this affects functionality or not. 

CC @ssanderson

issue_title: namespace conflict with https://pypi.python.org/pypi/serial/0.0.41?
issue_body: Both pyserial and serial-0.0.41 (which is a dependency of requests, and other packages) both install to the same 'serial' directory in site-packages and clobber each other.  Is there a sophisticated way to make pyserial install to a pyserial namespace without forking and changing the code itself?

issue_title: Trying to select two entire rows in table view ends up selecting many more...
issue_body: On macOS High Sierra under Debug build. It works if I end at the second column but I can't select two entire rows (including row numbers) regardless of whether I start at the top left or the bottom right. Image attached.

issue_title: OpenCL | | syCL | | HIP support ?
issue_body:
The Apple i/GPU usebase is mostly made of Intel and AMD. Yet you've chosen to follow that irational trend of going for the ridiculously little minority (watch the JPR stats) that are nvidia gpus, because 1) they invest ton of money for brainwashing developpers, 2) some tools like Tensorflow do not yet support the standards because of the 1) reason.
The second reason would be valid for à little developer team that has no the choice, BUT you are APPLE, you have unlimited money and you are the creators of OpenCL !!
If you weren't aware, AMD has designed an impressive tool for translating existing modern CUDA code in OpenCL code. Often it take less than one week to convert à project with this tool. 
https://github.com/ROCm-Developer-Tools/HIP
I'm not even talking of the fact that AMD hardware is far more suited for machine learning than Nvidia because for example it support full FP16... 
So, will you choose the rational way that let choice to the consumer, and to the PC maker ?

issue_title: Consider EVM instructio EXP in Integer Overflow and Underflow
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
Mythril doesn't detect overflow by exponentiation.

**To Reproduce**

Create solidity contract: 

```solidity

pragma solidity ^0.4.23;

contract IntegerOverflow {
    uint public count = 1;

    function run(uint256 input) public {
        count ^= input;
    }
}
```

compile it, and then run `myth` on it.

**Expected behavior**

```
==== Integer Overflow ====
Type: Warning
Contract: IntegerOverflow
Function name: **ambiguous** r
PC address: 174
A possible integer overflow exists in the function `**ambiguous** r`.
The addition, multiplication or exponentiation may result in a value higher than the maximum representable integer.
--------------------
In file: test.sol:1

count ^= input
```

**Desktop:**
 - OS: Ubuntu
 - Version Bionic

Thanks in advance.
Sako

issue_title: uncaught series exception
issue_body:
(I have to stack things up here to not forget them...)
Somewhere in symgenine.py this exception is not caught:
```
In [2]: from symengine import *

In [3]: x=Symbol('x')                      

In [4]: series(1/cos(x/log(x)), x, 0)
Out[4]: 
         2            4   
        x          5⋅x         ⎛ 6⎞
1 + ───────── + ────────── + O⎝x ⎠
         2            4       
    2⋅log (x)   24⋅log (x)  

In [5]: series(1/cos(x/log(x)), x, 0, method='symengine')
Exception (fmpq_poly_log_series). Constant term != 1.

Abort caught. Printing stacktrace:

Traceback (most recent call last):

Done.
Aborted
```
This SymPy test case is also interesting because, although univariate and having `log(x)`, SymPy solves it by leaving the log as were it another symbol. This shows the cases nomally throwing exceptions with `URatP...` can be treated by other means.

issue_title: Remove `Subs.__cinit__` and `Derivative.__cinit__`
issue_body:
Reason is the same as why we don't have `Add.__cinit__`.
`Subs.__cinit__` may create non-canonical expressions. Solution would be to remove this method and add a `Subs.__init__` to `symengine.sympy_compat`.

issue_title: BV2Int Overflow Problems?
issue_body:
So I've been looking at converting a BitVector object (with it's own constraints already), into an Integer object. However, there appears to be strangeness with the `BV2Int` method.

Example:
```python
In [39]:  x = BitVec('x', 3)

In [40]: x
Out[40]: x

In [41]:  prove(x <= 3)
proved

In [42]:  prove(BV2Int(x) <= 3)
counterexample
[x = 6]
```

My guess is z3 is allowing overflow in handling of BV2Int object, thus 6 would indeed be possible, however undesirable behavior in my case.

Is there a way to make it honor over/underflows?

issue_title: Java API equals() method of class "FuncDecl"
issue_body:
I find that in the Java API, each time the method getFuncDecl() in class Expr is called, a new instance of FuncDecl will be created and returned. However, the equals() method in FuncDecl basically returns the result of "this==o". As a result, two FuncDecl instances will be "not equal", even if they refer to the same function declaration. I guess it would be more reasonable if the equals() method returns "(this.getId()==((FuncDecl)o).getId)". Moreover, the hashCode() method might also need to be modified.

The class Symbol has a similar problem.

issue_title: Issues with quantification over several variables
issue_body:
I'm not sure that the problems I encounter are problems in Z3 per se, but I assume or at least hope that there are ways to avoid them. I'm looking for help in doing that.

My goal is to model the occurrence of an event over a period of time in order to be able to determine if it happens at a certain point that I'm asked about. For example, assuming that it is given that the event:
starts at  2000-05-12, 10:45:12, and
ends at:  2010-08-12, 14:53:52
If I'm asked whether it takes place at 2003-1-12 10:46:22, I should return 'yes'. 
If I'm asked whether it takes place at 2020-8-12 10:46:22, I should return 'no'. 

I define a predicate - f - of 6 variables - year, month, day, hour, minute and second - and asserts that it returns 'true' in the range of times when the event takes place, and 'false' outside of that range. 

(declare-fun f (Int Int Int Int Int Int) Bool)

(assert (forall ((y Int) (m Int) (d Int) (h Int) (mi Int) (s Int)) 
(=> (and 

(or 
(> y 2000) 
(and (= y 2000) (> m 5)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (> d 12)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (= d 12) (> h 10)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (= d 12) (= h 10) (> mi 45)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (= d 12) (= h 10) (= mi 45) (>= s 12)))

(or 
(< y 2010) 
(and (= y 2010) (< m 8)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (< d 22)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (= d 22) (< h 14)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (= d 22) (= h 14) (< mi 53)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (= d 22) (= h 14) (= mi 53) (<= s 52))) 

)

(= (f y m d h mi s) true))))

(assert (forall ((y Int) (m Int) (d Int) (h Int) (mi Int) (s Int)) 
(=> (or 
(< y 2000) 
(and (= y 2000) (< m 5)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (< d 12)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (= d 12) (< h 10)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (= d 12) (= h 10) (< mi 45)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (= d 12) (= h 10) (= mi 45) (< s 12))) 
(= (f y m d h mi s) false))))

(assert (forall ((y Int) (m Int) (d Int) (h Int) (mi Int) (s Int)) 
(=> (or 
(> y 2010) 
(and (= y 2010) (> m 8)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (> d 22)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (= d 22) (> h 14)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (= d 22) (= h 14) (> mi 53)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (= d 22) (= h 14) (= mi 53) (> s 52))) 
(= (f y m d h mi s) false))))

Then I can check whether the event takes place at a particular point that I'm asked about, for example, for 2003-1-12 10:46:22 I'm asserting:
(assert (f 2003 1 12 10 46 22))
This should result in SAT, which indicates that the event takes place at that point.

But for  2020-8-12 10:46:22:
(assert (f 2020 8 12 10 46 22))
I should get UNSAT, which indicates that the event doesn't take place at that point.

My problem is that even without the assertion about a specific point in time, it takes Z3 about 15 **minutes** to give a SAT answer. I don't know if this is a bug in one of its heuristics or an unavoidable limitation of being in an undecidable logic, but I noticed that if I reduce the modeling to 5 variables (i.e. avoiding seconds), it takes about 15 **seconds** to give a SAT answer, and for 4 variables (avoiding minutes and seconds) it's almost immediate. So the processing time is definitely related to the number of variables being quantified, but I have to model all 6 variables.

I'm trying to find ways to optimize my modeling or Z3 settings in order to get a quicker response. As for Z3 - I tried various settings such as:
(set-option :smt.mbqi true)
(set-option :model.compact true)
which didn't make a difference, and
(set-option :smt.mbqi false)
which causes Z3 to return 'unknown' immediately. 

I'd really appreciate any feedback and suggestions how to optimize this modeling or how to configure Z3 for obtaining a quicker response in this use-case.

Attached: a script with 5 variables that returns SAT in 15 seconds and a script with 6 variables that returns SAT in about 15 minutes.

[tests.zip](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/269106/tests.zip)

issue_title: seq.at without length constraints sometimes produces incorrect models
issue_body:
Using seq.at in equality assertions seems to sometimes result in incorrect models, for example when there is no assertion about the length of the target sequence:

```
(declare-fun xs () (Seq Int))
(assert (= (seq.at xs 0) (seq.unit 42)))
;; Adding the following assertion results in a correct model:
;; (assert (> (seq.len xs) 0))
(check-sat)
(get-model)
```

The above program results in the following model:
```
(model 
  (define-fun xs () (Seq Int)
    (as seq.empty (Seq Int)))
)
```

However, `seq.empty` will not satisfy the above assertion. Interestingly, adding a length constraint like `(assert (= (seq.len xs) 0))` still yields `sat` as the result, with the same model. Is this a bug or did I miss some assumption about the use of sequence operations?

issue_title: Impossible to disable Cancel link
issue_body: The current form template API does not provide any mechanism to disable the *Cancel* link. We already have several forms in production whose *Cancel* links take you back to the same form you were on before.

issue_title: Docs of the CustomRecurrentLayer
issue_body:
In the docs the examples for the `CustomRecurrentLayer` and the docs don't match. Particularly - ``
```
input_to_hidden : :class:`lasagne.layers.Layer`
        :class:`lasagne.layers.Layer` instance which connects input to the
        hidden state (:math:`f_i`).  This layer may be connected to a chain of
        layers, which must end in a :class:`lasagne.layers.InputLayer` with the
        same input shape as `incoming`. 
```
However, in the examples to `incoming` has shape `(n_batch, n_steps, n_in)` while the bottom `InputLayer` of the `input_to_hidden` has shape `(None, n_in)`. The same applies an the example of convolutional recurrence where the `incoming` has shape `(n_batch, n_steps, n_channels, width, height)`, while the bottom of the `input_to_hidden` has shape `(None, n_channels, width, height)`. From this it seems that the `InputLayer` of the `input_to_hidden` must have shape `incoming.shape[1:]`.

Or is it intentional that it takes `None` at the start so that you flat out the batch and the input length?

issue_title: S3FS.remove('parent_directory/file') deletes both `file` and `parent_directory` when there is only one file in the directory
issue_body:
I've noticed that when there's only one file in a directory, running `botocore.s3.delete_object` deletes both the file and the parent directory. This is unexpected. I've raised an issue on the botocore list https://github.com/boto/botocore/issues/1275 but in the meantime I'd suggest modifying the implementation of `S3FS.remove` to something like
```
    def remove(self, path):
        self.check()
        _path = self.validatepath(path)
        _key = self._path_to_key(_path)
        info = self.getinfo(path)
        if info.is_dir:
            raise errors.FileExpected(path)

        # get around unexpected behaviour in S3
        # whereby deleting the last file in a directory
        # deletes the parent directory as well
        # https://github.com/boto/botocore/issues/1275
        parent = self.delimiter.join(_key.split(self.delimiter)[:-1])
        recreate_parent = len(self.listdir(parent)) == 1
        self.client.delete_object(
            Bucket=self._bucket_name,
            Key=_key
        )
        if recreate_parent:
            try:
                 self.makedir(parent)
            except Exception:  # use a more specific exception here
                 pass

```

issue_title: condition_yaw doesn't work without a call to send_global_velocity
issue_body:
I was testing out the `condition_yaw` in the docs and it while the command would send a MAVLink message, and the drone (SITL) would respond with success, the drone would not yaw (as indicated by the `vehicle.attitude.yaw` field). However, if I precede yaw commands with a `send_global_velocity(0, 0, 0)` command (again, taken from the docs), the drone will rotate successfully. 

I am executing these commands in GUIDED mode. As far as I can tell, there doesn't seem to be a mention of this requirement in the docs (I guessed at it since the yaw examples included `send_global_velocity`).

issue_title: Something we don't monitor blocking arming on early startup
issue_body:
The normal "arm and takeoff" code (as per [simple_goto.py](https://github.com/dronekit/dronekit-python/blob/master/examples/simple_goto/simple_goto.py#L29) example) checks that state is not initialising and that we have a good GPS fix. Then it changes the mode to GUIDED and tries to arm the vehicle. 

On the Windows Copter 3.3 dronekit-sitl build this script successfully arms and takes off if you've waited a bit or already connected to mission planner. However if you start the script shortly after the simulator it never arms. This means that there is something the autopilot is waiting on before we can arm - if you call the command to ARM before this "thing" completes the device will never arm.

If you add a delay of around 8 seconds after the vehicle changes to guided, but before calling ARM then the vehicle will ARM. If you just call ARM repeatedly in the loop while waiting for it to arm this also works. To me this again indicates that there is something that we need to wait on before calling ARM.

Any ideas what it might be? The "easy" solution for the example is to just set arm to true in the loop - but this is pretty crap - much better to identify whatever pre-arm condition is stopping us from proceeding.

Thoughts?

Hello everybody and thanks for this great library.
I'm newbie in wavelets and have a question regarding SWT. I don't know if it's a bug or not, but why do I get some weird spikes at the end of sequence? Here is the plot and corresponding code:
![swt](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/510509/13669752/8d36f952-e6d6-11e5-9299-32a3002b65f3.png)

```
import pywt
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# generate some random signal 
x = np.arange(64)
s = np.sin(x/(2*np.pi)) + np.cos(x/(5*np.pi)) + 0.2*np.random.randn(len(x))

# full SWT decomposition
(A2,D2),(A1,D1) = pywt.swt(s, 'haar', 2)
plt.plot(A2, 'b')

(A2,D2),(A1,D1) = pywt.swt(s[:32], 'haar', 2)

issue_title: Add a BagofBonds structure featurizer
issue_body:
This would take in a structure and retain a list that contains the number of bonds (probably as fraction). For example, if a structure had 2 Li-O bonds and 3 O-P bonds, it could return [0.4, 0.6] as the featurizer value and ["Li-O", "Li-P"] as the feature labels.

Some aspects to note:
* You probably want to allow any pymatgen NearNeighbor class to be used as the bond detection method. Some presets might help with usage
* If you are featurizing an entire data frame, you need the feature labels to match and be a concatenation of all the bonds in all the various structures in the data frame. Also some complications if you fit a model and then predict a structure for which there are no bonds in the training set. Some hashing tricks from text mining might help with this. Talk to AJ if you are implementing and this is confusing to you.

issue_title: get_qery method assuming query is an attribute, fails when its a @property
issue_body:
The get_query methods from both SQLAlchemyConnectionField and SQLAlchemyObjectType
assume that the Base model has an attribute **_query_** but if this is a property then it will fail. 
I have made this changes to fix it in SQLAlchemyConnectionField:
```python
class SQLAlchemyConnectionField(Connection):
    @classmethod
    def get_query(cls, model, info, **kwargs):
        q = get_query(model, info.context)
        return q() if callable(q) else q
```

But is probably better to do it in the utils module:
```python
def get_query(model, context):
    query = getattr(model, 'query', None)
    if not query:
        session = get_session(context)
        if not session:
            raise Exception('A query in the model Base or a session in the schema is required for querying.\n'
                            'Read more http://graphene-python.org/docs/sqlalchemy/tips/#querying')
        query = session.query(model)
    else:
        query = query() if callable(query) else query
return query
```

issue_title: Alpine Linux & Musl libc compatibility: _IOC_SIZEBITS undefined

I am experimenting with OpenThread in Docker using Alpine Linux, which uses musl libc. Compiling the unmodified sources resulted in an error about _IOC_SIZEBITS not being defined. Googling this error led me to the following page and prompted my solution included below: https://www.spinics.net/lists/linux-spi/msg08503.html

```
--- spi-hdlc-adapter.c    2018-05-06 16:36:19.000000000 +0200
+++ spi-hdlc-adapter.c  2018-05-15 19:14:09.000000000 +0200
@@ -50,6 +50,7 @@
 #include <sys/ioctl.h>
 #include <sys/file.h>

+#include <linux/ioctl.h>
 #include <linux/spi/spidev.h>

 #if HAVE_EXECINFO_H
```

I'm open to submitting this change as a PR, but maybe it's simpler for someone with pushing rights to just make this commit. Either way is fine.

Just couldn't get barcodes to print reliably or completely on the TM81.  
Looking at the docs 
http://nicholas.piasecki.name/blog/wp-content/uploads/2009/12/ESC-POS-Command-Gu
ide.pdf  printing a barcode 
to print 

1. ASCII GS k md1...dk NUL 
 Hex. 1D 6B md1...dk NUL 
 Decimal 29 107 md1...dk NUL 
2. ASCII GS k m nd1...dk 
 Hex. 1D 6B m nd1...dk

issue_title: Force file_type on read
issue_body:
Hey,
So working with files ending with ``.txt`` that are actually ``.csv`` files.
This is in fact the standard in [ Example GTFS Feed ](https://developers.google.com/transit/gtfs/examples/gtfs-feed) which are used by google and governments.

So I need a way to read a file ending in ``.txt`` as a ``.csv``.

I see here in the code that there is a use of ``file_type``, but it would not work if there is a ``file_name`` already defined. Is this a bug or covering a use-case I don't know?
Can I PR something that uses ``force_file_type`` or something like that?
```python
class FileSourceInfo(SourceInfo):
    """Plugin description for a file source"""
    def is_my_business(self, action, **keywords):
        status = SourceInfo.is_my_business(self, action, **keywords)
        if status:
            file_name = keywords.get("file_name", None)
            if file_name:
                if is_string(type(file_name)):
                    file_type = find_file_type_from_file_name(file_name,
                                                              action)
                else:
                    raise IOError("Wrong file name")
            else:

            status = self.can_i_handle(action, file_type)
        return status
```

issue_title: file descriptors are not closed
issue_body:
For example, when running the following code on Linux:

```python
import os

import pyexcel

pyexcel.get_book_dict(file_name='test.xlsx')

fd_dir = '/proc/%u/fd' % os.getpid()
for fd_name in os.listdir(fd_dir):
    print(fd_name, '-> ', end='')
    try:
        print(os.readlink('%s/%s' % (fd_dir, fd_name)))
    except FileNotFoundError:
        print()
```

The last file descriptor open point to `test.xlsx`.

Beside the file descriptor leak, this is really problematic in Windows, as it make it impossible to concurrently modify the spreadsheet in Office when it has been read in another (still running) application.

issue_title: do we support utf-8?
issue_body:
Hello, thank you for your contribution.

I performed some tests with classification on the Chinese text, while an error occurred as follow:
* terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::bad_array_new_length'
*  what():  std::bad_array_new_length
* Aborted (core dumped)

Through many debugging， the error is located in the code of function of save and load in dictionary.cc.
In function Dictionary::save, 0 is saved as the end of word.data, as following code.
* ofs.write(e.word.data(), e.word.size() * sizeof(char));
* ofs.put(0);

It's ok when the text is written in English, while it maybe incompatible with other languages.
Will the software support utf-8 in future versions?

issue_title: Feature: Remove 3600 seconds duration limit
issue_body:
Hi.

If I try to set the duration parameter to more than 3600 seconds, an error is returned:
> Duration must be less than or equal to 3600


But, from what I understand from the documentation, the real limit for most cases is 129600 seconds, given that the 3600 seconds limitation only apply to root accounts:

> The GetSessionToken action must be called by using the long-term AWS security credentials of the AWS account or an IAM user. Credentials that are created by IAM users are valid for the duration that you specify, from 900 seconds (15 minutes) up to a maximum of 129600 seconds (36 hours), with a default of 43200 seconds (12 hours); credentials that are created by using account credentials can range from 900 seconds (15 minutes) up to a maximum of 3600 seconds (1 hour), with a default of 1 hour.

(Reference: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/STS/latest/APIReference/API_GetSessionToken.html)

Can we remove this limitation, then?

Thanks!

issue_title: Need nics info call on containers like we have on nodes
Mailgun: certain metadata keys can break tracking webhooks
issue_body:
[Discovered while investigating #76]

Because of the way Mailgun merges user-variables into its webhook posts, using metadata keys that conflict with [Mailgun event parameters](http://mailgun-documentation.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user_manual.html#tracking-opens) can confuse Anymail's event parsing and lead to unexpected results. For example, if you send this message:

```python
    AnymailMessage(
        # ...
        to=["customer@example.com"],
        metadata={"event": "holiday campaign", "recipient": "vip"}
    ).send()
```

The the tracking handler will receive incorrect information for all tracking events (because "event" and "recipient" are Mailgun event parameters):

```python
    @receiver(tracking)
    def handle_event(sender, event, esp_name, **kwargs):
        # the event_type should be "received" or "opened", etc., but...
        print(event.event_type)  # "unknown" (for every event about this message)
        # the recipient should be "customer@example.com", but...
        print(event.recipient)  # "vip"
```

It looks like Mailgun supplies both its event parameter field and the user-variable field in the post data, so they're both available in Django's POST QueryDict:
```python
        print(event.esp_event.getlist('recipient'))  # ["customer@example.com", "vip"]
```
Just need to figure out how to reliably split Mailgun's event params from the metadata ones. (The merge order is not consistent between fields.)

Affects all versions of Anymail through 1.0.

issue_title: macOS BootstrapMac fails to install pip and virtualenv due to permission error
issue_body:
When running `letsencrypt-auto --help --debug` `BootstrapMac` function attempts to install `pip` and `virtualenv` with this code:

```
curl --silent --show-error --retry 5 https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py |  python
pip install virtualenv
```
Since `/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/` is owned by `root` with 755 permissions installation fails with permission error.

One way to "fix" it is to chmod -R 777  site-packages packages directory as described in this blog [post](https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/complete-guide-to-install-ssl-certificate-on-your-os-x-server-hosted-website/15005).

Another way is to add $SUDO to install commands:

```
curl --silent --show-error --retry 5 https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py |  $SUDO python
$SUDO pip install virtualenv
```

This appears to work (tested on Sierra 10.12.3 with both MacPorts and HomeBrew).

issue_title: Create blacklist for configuration directives we can't safely copy over
issue_body:
There are some configuration directives that break when duplicated. They are used somewhat rarely, and in very specific configurations. Instead of doing guesswork and trying to modify the directives, we should instead create a blacklisting mechanism that ignores (or comments off) the problematic statements / blocks. 

If directives are disabled for the newly created HTTPS configuration, we should display a meaningful message pointing the user to the configuration file(s) that need manual modification.

Examples: #2726 #1820

issue_title: Signals during error handling
issue_body:
For those not familiar, `certbot/error_handler.py` can be used to execute cleanup code in case an exception or signal is encountered. Currently, however, the error handler can still be interrupted. For example, suppose the user hits ctrl+c to interrupt Certbot. If the user hits ctrl+c again while the error handler is running, it is interrupted and exits without performing all of the necessary cleanup code.

We could potentially ignore such signals during error handling. If we want to do this, I think the best way is before running any cleanup functions, the `ErrorHandler` uses `signal` to ignore signals such as `SIGINT`. After running the cleanup code, the original signal handlers should be restored.

issue_title: Unable to serialize object Salesforce
issue_body:
I need to serialize object Salesforce so that I can store it in Django's request.session for repeated use. However, neither JSON- nor Pickle-based serializers can handle it. I ended up adding following code to `Salesforce.__getattr__()`
```python
        # fix that enables pickle to work
        if name.startswith('__') and name.endswith('__'):
            return super(Salesforce, self).__getattr__(name)
```
to eliminate problems caused by special attributes, but there may be a better solution.

issue_title: catch more exceptions
issue_body:
Somewhat open ended.

Currently:

- CoT verification timeouts aren't caught: this results in scriptworker crashing and the task claim expiring, which is a slow way of rerunning the task. Ideally we'd catch the timeout and retry or explicitly exit `intermittent-task`.

- CoT artifact unicode results in scriptworker crashing. #216

- We have some uncaught exceptions while running a task - some around max timeout. Ideally we'd catch the currently-uncaught and gracefully either exit the task or otherwise handle it. This might involve `worker-shutdown` or exponential retry backoff; either way it would be wonderful to upload the logs-in-progress.

### Mode of operation

issue_title: Add http endpoint for the token swap
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Raiden needs to expose an atomic swap interface.

## Solution

For the atomic swap execution one node will initiate the swap, the other node will preset the expected rate and verify once the swap message is received, the alternative approach is to not preset the rate but to inquire (through a callback mechanism) if the received swap is valid, this second approach has the down sides of increasing latency of the mediated transfer and would force the REST API to have an additional end point for emitting events to do the inquiry.

Note: For atomic swaps both nodes need to agree on an exchange rate, the process through which the exchanges are agreed upon is not defined by Raiden.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Add an endpoint for presetting the expected rate and being the initiator of a token swap. `api/<version>/token_swaps/<identifier>`
  - This end point blocks until the swap is completed or a timeout happens, the lock expiration is not defined at the moment of the call so a different value needs to be used.
  - User needs to do a `PUT` request to the endpoint with the swap object as json data:
     ```json
     {
         "role": "maker",
         "receiving_amount": <amount:int>,
         "sending_amount":  <amount:int>,
         "receiving_token": <address:hexstring>,
         "sending_token": <address:hexstring>
      }
     ```
- [ ] Use the exact same endpoint as above but provide the `taker` role in the json data in order to start a selected token swap.
  - This end point blocks until the swap is completed or a timeout happens, the endpoint doesn't need to block until the lock expiration since this is the node controlling the secret.

The arguments `receiving_token`, `receiving_amount`, `sending_token`, and `sending_amount` are in respect to each node, e.g. (Note the parameters are reversed for each node.): The `identifier` is a pre-agreed upon identifier for the swap.

1. Alice will send 10 A to Bob, Bob will send 15 B in return.
1.1 Bob will configure it's Raiden node to expect the swap where he receives 10 A and sends 15 B.
1.1 Alice will start the swap by sending 10 A and receiving the 15 B.

issue_title: Import and export to DICOM, while maintaing tags
issue_body:
Imagine user who loads CTX cube from DICOM, manipulates the cube and dumps it to DICOM again.
In such case new DICOM will get completely different tags than the original one and in consequence might lead to problems in loading such DICOM in TPSes like Eclipse.

An easy solution to that would be to keep reasonable subset of DICOM tags in pytrip data cubes (i.e. as metadata) - to be used for further DICOM export.

To be discussed - how to handle such tags when adding/subtracting cubes.

issue_title: Rename sos execute to sos task?
issue_body:
Interface wise, we currently have

```
sos execute task_id
sos execute task_id --query
sos execute task_id --kill
```

but 

```
sos task execute task_id
sos task query task_id
sos task kill task_id
```

might read better.

We coud have

```
sos execute task_id
sos query task_id
sos kill task_id
```

but not sure if we should devote three (or more) subcommands for external tasks.

Note that `query` and `kill` are mostly for internal use (called by `run`) but users could use them from command line and there can be addons that use these tools, for example, to provide GUI for job monitoring.

issue_title: Field.build_vocab breaks when using dict fields to load CSV
issue_body:
Hi!

Using the master version of this repo, I tried to create a `TabularDataset` using a `dict` to load specific columns from a CSV. While the `TabularDataset` object was initialised fine (unlike the PyPI version, where using a dict for fields ends in a crash), trying to build vocab using those fields does not in fact build the vocab.

Example:

```
sentence = data.Field(lower=True, eos_token='<END>',
                                   include_lengths=True, batch_first=True,
                                   tokenize=word_tokenize)
fields = {'sentence': ('sentence', sentence), 
              ... }  # some other fields here 
train_data = data.TabularDataset(path=str(train_path), format='csv',
                             fields=fields, skip_header=False)
sentence.build_vocab(sentiment_train, max_size=80000,
                     vectors=GloVe(name='6B', dim=100))  # doesn't actually build the vocab
```

The resulting vocab only has the 3 tokens UNK, PAD, and EOS, and nothing else.

I have found other bugs on the master version, so perhaps this functionality is still in progress and I shouldn't attempt to use it yet?

issue_title: Stale partition URL after delete/create partition
issue_body:
### Actual behavior

If a "Create NIC" operation is issued on a partition that was just deleted and created again, it fails with HTTP status 404, reason 1, message "Not found or not authorized". The reason for that is that the URL of the deleted partition is still used.

The sequence of HMC operations for reproducing the failure is:

- Logon
- List CPCs (and pick one)
- List Partitions (filter by name)
- Delete Partition
- Create Partition
- List partitions (filter by name)
- Create NIC on the resulting partition -> fails with 404,1

The WS API log on the HMC shows that the Create NIC operation in its target URL uses the partition ID of the partition resource that was deleted, when it should have used the newly created partition resource.

### Expected behavior

Use ID of newly created partition.

### Execution environment

* zhmcclient version: 0.12.0
* Operating system (type+version):
* HMC version: 2.13.1
* CPC version: 2.13.1

issue_title: (deleted)
Plasma manager being marked dead because call to Create takes 60 seconds.
issue_body:
While debugging #670, I came across the following problem.

The setup is the following.

- One head node (m4.16xlarge)
- 99 other nodes (c4.xlarge)
- Run the following workload on the head node

    ```python
    @ray.remote
    def f():
        return 1

    %time l = ray.get([f.remote() for _ in range(10000000)])
    ```

After several minutes, the plasma manager logs a fatal error for taking too long to send a heartbeat. Adding in some timing statements, the problem seems to be that the call

```c++
  Status s = conn->manager_state->plasma_conn->Create(
      object_id, data_size, NULL, metadata_size, &(buf->data));
```

in `process_data_request` in `plasma_manager.cc`, which creates an object in the object store (and which is a blocking call), takes **60 seconds** https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/96962cdee0c6803695505dfb3c3cdcc451ce7a5f/src/plasma/plasma_manager.cc#L911 (this is an absurd amount of time). Note that at this point in the script, that line has only been called about 700 times. All prior calls to that line took around 0ms, and all subsequent calls to that line took around 6ms. Note that the number 700 seems very small. This could be because the vast majority of tasks are getting executed locally.

issue_title: Prototype experimental ray.status API or design alternative API.
issue_body:
One API that may be useful for some workloads is the ability to call `ray.status(object_id)` and within a task to be able to call `ray.update_status(value)` or something like that.

For hyperparameter search, this could notify the driver about progress. It could also be used to make decisions about cancelling certain tasks or actors (see #854).

This introduces challenges for handling failures and recovering prior state.

cc @imzhenyu

issue_title: LD_LIBRARY_PATH not set on workers on different machine.
issue_body:
Starting Ray on a cluster of 3 nodes (Ubuntu 16), I encountered the following.

```python
import ray
import os

ray.init(redis_address="...")

@ray.remote
def f():
  node_ip_address = ray.services.get_node_ip_address()
  if "LD_LIBRARY_PATH" in os.environ:
    return os.environ["LD_LIBRARY_PATH"], node_ip_address
  return "", node_ip_address

results = ray.get([f.remote() for _ in range(1000)])
```

Then `set(results)` looked something like this

```
{('', '172.31.16.145'),
 ('', '172.31.29.31'),
 ('/usr/local/lib64:/usr/local/lib64::/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/openravepy/_openravepy_', '172.31.38.127')}
```

So locally the `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` was set for each worker, but on the remote machines it was not.

The reason is that parallel-ssh does not run `source ~/.bashrc` so a bunch of environment variables are not set. We should change the cluster documentation instructions to run `source ~/.bashrc` in the script that starts Ray on the workers.

By the way, the symptom of this problem was that a remote function definition failed to be defined on the workers on the remote machines because it needed something on the `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`. The actual error message (when defining the remote function) was something like

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ray-0.0.1-py2.7.egg/ray/worker.py", line 1022, in fetch_and_register_remote_function
    function = pickling.loads(serialized_function)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/pickle.py", line 1388, in loads
    return Unpickler(file).load()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/pickle.py", line 864, in load
    dispatch[key](self)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/pickle.py", line 1139, in load_reduce
    value = func(*args)
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/cloudpickle/cloudpickle.py", line 717, in subimport
    __import__(name)
  File "/home/ubuntu/dex-net/deps/meshpy/meshpy/__init__.py", line 1, in <module>
    import meshrender
ImportError: libboost_numpy.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
```

Note: I just tried starting Ray on the non-head nodes by using

```
parallel-ssh -h workers.txt -P -I < start_worker.sh
```

where `start_worker.sh` manually sets the `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` via

```
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/local/lib64:/usr/local/lib64::/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/openravepy/_openravepy_
ray/scripts/start_ray.sh --num-workers=40 --redis-address=172.31.38.127:6666
```

and that solved the problem.

However, if instead of manually setting `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`, I instead do `source /home/ubuntu/.bashrc`, that somehow does not help. I'm not sure why.

issue_title: Remote function cannot recursively call itself.
issue_body:
Consider the following script.

```python
import ray

ray.init()

@ray.remote
def f():
  return ray.get(f.remote())

f.remote()
```

When `f.remote()` is called, the following error is printed.

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<ipython-input-3-6f5c275d0ce3>", line 3, in f
NameError: name 'f' is not defined
```

This worked at some point but seems to have gotten broken. We weren't testing recursively defining remote functions because defining remote functions in this manner inside of the Python unit testing framework didn't work. However, we should add tests for this behavior and just not use the unit testing framework for that part.

issue_title: Issue with the ingestion of UInt16 and UInt32 dtypes
issue_body:
When trying to load an ingested dataset with a dtype of either UInt16 or UInt32, the following error comes up:
![dc_load](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/22379995/25388543/22046c32-299c-11e7-94a7-47c3260c88d5.PNG)

It seems that the NetCDF files generated during ingestion are trying to be read in as datatype 8/9, which correspond to the unsupported CInt types - [seen here](https://github.com/mapbox/rasterio/blob/master/rasterio/dtypes.py#L26). The dtype is set to UInt16 in both the product definition and the ingestion configuration file, and running gdalinfo on the .nc file reports it as a UInt16:
![gdalinfo](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/22379995/25388658/6d3f5856-299c-11e7-94b9-b075fff2ffdc.PNG)

It seems to be a rasterio issue, as trying to open the dataset results in the following warning:
![rasterio_open](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/22379995/25388717/8e30205e-299c-11e7-8a35-55ae372531df.PNG)

The error in the DC occurs because it won't cast the default Float32 type back to whatever is set in the ingestion configuration file. Is this a rasterio issue or does this have something to do with how the DC writes the netCDF files? I can load in the source dataset (GeoTiff) as UInt16/32 without any issues.

issue_title: ITEM_LOOKUP_FIELD does not properly inherit its value from ID_FIELD
issue_body:
The ITEM_LOOKUP_FIELD value is retrieved during the `registerSchema` call from the _Eve defaults_, instead of the eve settings you actually pass to the application.

Obviously there is something wrong with the way this is implemented: `registerSchema` apparently needs to be called _before_ the eve application is created and its output is used to fill-in the app settings, so there is no way for it to access the eve application settings which only exist after the app is created (...). The fact that `registerSchema` even tries to access the app configuration is wrong.

Also, I am not sure why you need to retrieve the value of ITEM_LOOKUP_FIELD on schema registration in the first place.

This is also mentioned in issue #42.
